top_profane.text:
Garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, garbage, GARBAGE.

Some people need to touch the stove for themselves to see that it's hot, and I am one of them. Despite the reviews here and on every printing site, I still had to try one of these. Did I get it to work? A couple of times...but that was only through sheer luck, and I'm still not sure if the joins would hold up to going through the extruder. Of the five "successful" welds I got (out of at least a hundred tries), three of them snapped rolling the filament back on to the line.

Besides the fact that there are no instructions, the design concept itself is flawed. The IDEA is that you get the filament melty, then push the pieces together and then pull through the sizing block. The problem is, unless the filament is completely straight, it's not going to thread from the heating gap into the sizing hole. What kinds of filament will you be trying to join? The last bit on the spool. What is the last bit on the spool always like? Yup, super tightly wound and curved. Even if you straighten that out, what does hot filament do? It droops.

So IF you have someone to help you (two hands to hold the filament pieces, one to hold the lighter and maybe one to hold the welder itself) and IF you get everything lined up and IF you can then get just the right temp were the filament melts but doesn't catch fire and then IF you get it correctly into the sizer.....you MIGHT have a weld that can go through your machine. Or it will just snap when feeding. Who knows.

For the time and effort I put into this, I could have just bought a spool of rainbow filament and come out ahead. Save your scrap filament for welding parts with a 3D pen, or get a direct drive extruder that can feed short pieces.

As for this tool...it's too soot-stained and burned to return, so it's become the first tool I've ever had that won't end up sitting around forever and went straight in the trash.

Don't be like me. I touched the stove. It's hot. Stay away from it.
top_profane.title: Utter garbage

top_profane.text:
Let's be honest... this is just a stainless steel trash can. I will never understand the high price that Simple Human has attached to this product. It is IMHO very overpriced for a trash can. For this amount, you would think that it was a motion-sense trash can. Sadly, it isn't!

The brushed stainless steel trash can is beautiful to look at. The stainless steel is not of the shiny variety and it is indeed fingerprint-proof. Fingerprint-proof does not mean grease, oil or any other disgusting type of 'proof. Within one day of letting our three kids around it and it needed to be thoroughly wiped down.

The 'touch bar' is very reminiscent of the type of metal bars that are on school doors or certain lobbies. The bar requires a very light tap that is easy enough. The mouth is gloriously wide enough, where I am now able to carry over my cutting board and just swipe straight into the trash can. I no longer need to make a smaller garbage pile and then dispose into the larger bag at night. I am not sure of the long term durability of the 'touch bar'. It is a bit odd when you first step up to the trash can and realize that there isn't a foot pedal. We have had a couple of incidents where the trash can was rotated while the person searched for the foot pedal. Another family member gave up in 'locating' the foot pedal and went on to trying to pry the lid open manually. Whom would have ever thought that a small foot pedal would throw off so many innocent people. Fortunately, we have now taken the two minutes to demonstrate to the whole family how to properly operate a trash can. Yes, we have had a garbage clinic. Just like Home Depot has clinics. It takes a bit to get used to. I find that I kind of brush up against it with my upper thigh/hip area. It is extremely helpful for those times when your hands are full. Then again so are little foot pedals.

Simple Human does provide the consumer with 3 twelve gallon sample trash bags. For the amount that you are paying outright, I would have loved a full size box of trash bags! Come on Simple Human! Full size box of trash bags, not the piddly three that you are currently offering. The trash bags are an extremely TIGHT fit. At first it seemed idiotic and impossible, but now I do see the logic behind it. The bags do not slide down as others have done in the past. They also have a nice stretch to them so that the tight fit does not actually cause any damage to the bag that is filled with garbage. The bags are just too expensive for my taste, for what they are; GARBAGE bags. When properly placed the bag goes into a liner which assures you that there shouldn't be any disgusting stains or remnants in the can. The trash bag also 'disappears' because the stainless steel is higher than the plastic insert.

Recommend, but not highly. It is too expensive for just being a metal trash can. Pretty... yes, but not that pretty. The touch bar is great, but please be aware of the dimensions if you are purchasing for a small kitchen. There is no sliding it under a counter. Lost a star because nothing is perfect and current price point.
top_profane.title: Expensive metal...

top_profane.text: This stuff!!!!! I was on the verge of losing my mind because my cat had started pissing everywhere inside my house due to a random stray cat marking the outside of my house. I did everything I could to get rid of the odor and had even temporarily rehomed my cat until I could get the smell gone. I searched the internet for more solutions and read about enzymes. As I'm searching Amazon and reading reviews and ingredients, I came across this. Let me tell you... This stuff works!!!! I poured it straight into a spray bottle and went to town. I also had a blacklight I'd previously bought to hunt down cat piss so I waited til night time and went to town. I drenched the carpets, baseboards, walls and furniture that my kitty had claimed to be his own. It also says to keep the area wet for as long as possible, so I cut garbage bags into long strips and laid it down. I used both gallons all over my house, let it sit and then waited. The smell of the solution is very strong so I did have to wear a mask and would take breaks, but it was worth it. After about 48 hours of letting this stuff soak and dry, the strong smell goes away and my house doesn't smell like piss. I did wait a few weeks to write my review as I wanted to make sure the piss smell was really gone and not just masked like the other cleaners. I've gotten down on my hands and knees, put my face to the carpet and walls and there is no cat piss!!!! I will buy more to do my office as well because he had marked a few times in there and I ran out of this product because this stuff really worked! I've also been able to bring kitty back home and he has not pissed one time on anything. And I'm kind of convinced that the stray cat outside has limited his visits because he's not competing with my inside cat with a pissing war! I've told all my friends who have pets about this stuff and definitely look forward to using it on the rest of the house!
top_profane.title: Got cat piss!? Than you need this stuff!!!!!

top_profane.text:
Is it weird to be in love with a trash can?

For 7 years, I've been hanging on to my old trash can. I never really enjoyed my old trash can (I found it at Walmart and it was the cheapest one available) but I just cringe at the thought of paying $100 for something that holds trash! Well my old trash can was in pretty bad shape. It wasn't really tough to begin with and after 7 years of abuse, the handle had long broken off, the bottom was coming undone, but it still technically "worked". Well a few weeks ago I took the trash out and when I came back home my trash was gone, but so was my trash can. Now I suppose it's possible that someone stole my poor little trash can but I just don't see why when my neighbors have definitely got some better options. My best guess is that my trash guys decided that my trash can needed to go.

Fast forward to the now, tomorrow morning is trash day and I'm a little excited. I took the trash out tonight because I can't bring myself to wake up at 5:00 am when my trash guys get here. I personally love that the trash can is yellow. I think it really stands out and if someone does try to take it I think it would be easy to find. Wheeling my trash out was so lovely! Granted I haven't had a handle on my old trash can in years, but I'm pretty sure it didn't feel this great. The handle is so sturdy and the wheels are big and it's just so easy to maneuver.

And you really can't beat the price. At Walmart and Home Depot, the trash cans comparable to this size were almost double the price.
top_profane.title: Looooove my trash can!

top_profane.text:
Every once in a while there's a product that does exactly what it claims to do plus makes the actual task at hand easier to accomplish.

What I really like about this trash can:
+ This can weighs a very respectable 9-lbs empty and seems to be relatively wind and dog resistant. I have an impossibly brainy medium poodle that views trash cans as puzzles to be solved and its contents evenly distributed all over the house and yard. I will report back on how this touch-bar trash can compares to a pedal-can in regards to poodle-proofiness.
+ This brushed stainless trash can is fingerprint-proof as claimed as well as dog "nose-snail" resistant.
+ The touch bar is perfectly tuned to be easy enough to push but not loose enough that the slightest bump will activate the spring-lid.
+ The narrow-but-deep configuration makes this trash can easy to fit in small spaces, yet the height ensures that there is ample room or a decent amount of trash. This can measures 28"Tall x 16.9"Deep x 10.2"Wide. My litmus test for a good sized opening is that it must be able to easily take a small/personal sized pizza box, and this does.
+ There is an easily removeable inner bucket
+ The inner bucket has a small smooth round hole on one of the sides. This hole is for the insertion of a twisted-to-fit non-simplehuman garbage liner so that the liners fits smoothly this trash can. When using a simplehuman liner, there is no need to use this hole. Please see my uploaded photos.
+ There are a series of holes along the lower 2 sides of the inner bucket which serve 2 purposes: the first is as a handhold when washing or dumping contents out of the inner bucket, and the second is that it allows airflow so that a new liner does not puff up when it is first inserted into the inner bucket.
+ There is a plastic knob on the bottom of each side of the trash can. simple human calls this a "bucket park" and its mean to prop up the removable inner bucket so you can pull the liner off and tie it up during bag change.
+ The lid itself by design is a stay-open lid, unlike a pedal can where one must keep their foot on the pedal.
+ The lid part simply lifts off easily for bag change.

What I don't like about this trash can:
- The nature of a spring-loaded top x touch bar opening is that the top of the can is not sealed to prevent odors. This means that anything smelly must immediately go out to the dumpster. The size of this can and its liner seems wasteful when small stinky items such as a garlic peel or an onion skin or two requires immediately outside disposal.
- The company's perfectly fitted custom liners are not biodegradeable
- The liners are not cheap in any of the 50, 200 or 400-packs.
- The trash can itself seems quite expensive.

Extras:
* Perfectly fitted liners. simplehuman has designed trash can liners to fit their products perfectly. The trash can liner size for this trash can is size M trash bags that measure 30"T x 22"W. The Simple Human liners are not gusseted and are just a simple rectangle.
* simplehuman size M liners are available on Amazon in the 50-count pack and on the simplehuman website in a 200-count pack and a 400-count pack.
* There is an easy to use trash can liner finder on the company's website. You can search by liner size or by the model/style of trash can you have.

Overall a very well thought-out product. My only real disappointment is that the company did not make their custom fitted liners biodegradable. I will also report back when I find a suitably sized biodegradeable liner.
top_profane.title: Surprisingly well thought-out

top_profane.text:
I bought one of these from another retailer, and I highly recommend everyone buy something else. This is the most useless, user unfriendly, and absolute garbage 3d printer I've ever worked with.

First, the bed WILL. NOT. LEVEL. I've watched a dozen different videos and read dozens of different threads on setting up this piece of crap. Literally none of it works. I've tried using a half dozen different bed leveling test prints about thirty times. ONE came out the way it was supposed to, after I'd screwed around with the garbage printer for about 15 hours. So, to make sure, I immediately ran the same print again without changing anything. Simply started the print a second time. IT FAILED. Completely. Didn't adhere ANY filament to the build plate. Let me repeat that. I ran a test print. It came out as it should. I immediately ran THE SAME PRINT WITHOUT TOUCHING ANYTHING BUT THE CONTROLS TO START THE SAME PRINT AGAIN...and this piece of crap didn't put ANY filament on the build plate.

Second, after trying to figure out why the second test print failed, the extruder simply stopped extruding filament. Temperature was right. Everything was running properly (or at least as well as this piece of crap ever ran), but not a bit of filament was being extruded. So I attempted to remove the extruder nozzle. First, the garbage wrench that came with the printer BENT when I tried to remove the nozzle. Then, using another wrench, THE NOZZLE TWISTED OFF. So, now I have an even more useless piece of crap sitting in my home.

I've read countless different threads on this printer since I first tried to use it. Basically everyone says the only way to make it work is add a crap load of after market modifications to it and to flash new firmware to the stupid thing. So, basically, you can't actually use this printer as it's sold...you have to do extensive modification to it to make it do what it's supposed to do out of the box.

I'll never buy anything from Creality again.
top_profane.title: Absolutely Garbage Printer

top_profane.text: I was very hesitant about buying this garbage can due to a lack of reviews. Also very similar styles of garbage cans had terrible reviews on Amazon about how cheap they were and easy to break. I decided to take a chance on this one especially with how low the price was and the fact that it came with garbage bags. The price of this can was crazy cheap especially when you consider that the garbage bags alone tend to be more expensive than this can and bags combined was. Well let me tell you, I was extremely surprised when I recieved this order and opened it. Not only was the garbage can absolutely adorable but it also was very well made and the plastic was not that cheap plastic that breaks easy. It's that durable plastic that lasts. I purchased the pastel blue can with the pastel yellow lid and the colors make it look so sweet. The back of the can has spikes/plates sticking out similar to how the back of a stegosaurus looked. On the lid of the can the whole front part flips back so you have a wide open area to put your garbage in it. I really appreciate that feature because a lot of the mini garbage cans have lids that open in a way that hardly leaves any room for you to out garbage in it. The wide open mouth of this lid makes it easy for lil ones to throw their garbage away and easy for adults to use as well. This can garbage can would be perfect for a child's room/desk area or even an adults desk area (I'm 38 and bought it for my desk). The only negative thing I have to say is that on the front of the lid above where the teeth are it kind of looks like the paint was not smoothed out and instead it looks like it was dripping down the lid and dried that way. Now this isn't something that's easily visible unless there is direct light shining on the area. You can't even feel those dripping lines. I didn't even notice it until I held it in direct sunlight. It's definitely not an issue that should discourage you from buying this can. It's just a very minor flaw but I wanted to be honest in my review. Lastly as you can see this can comes with 5 rolls of mini garbage bags. When I first saw these mini rolls I was super disappointed because I though there couldn't possibly be more than a couple bags each roll. Boy was I ever wrong. I decided to completely unroll one of the garbage bag rolls and count it. I was stunned to find out that each roll contained 25 garbage bags meaning you get 100 mini garbage bags. They fit absolutely perfectly in the can and don't feel like they're cheap or that they would tear easy. If you're like me and you don't change the bag everytime you empty the can, then these bags could easily last a year or potentially longer. I only change the bag if something messy got in it. If it's just tissues, paper or anything dry I'll dump the garbage into a larger bag and if the bag in the mini can still looks pretty clean then there really is no need to change it. For such a cheap price you really get a great deal on these bags and the can. My son absolutely loves my little can so now I have to buy one for him. He likes making the can eat his hot wheels cars 😂. Anyway this was such a hidden gem to find and if you're searching for a cute can then look no further and purchase this adorable dino garbage can!
top_profane.title: A hidden gem! Great quality and even better value!

top_profane.text:
My profession is custodial, so I’ve been around the Uline 32 gallon trash cans for quite some time. I’ve been in a hospital setting, retail setting, and school setting. Currently trying to work on a project and these are just perfect. They’re not so big, but not so small. I do have to bend over when adding a bag or taking it out, but I’m 5’10. Anyone over 6’0 I recommend investing in a 32 gallon or an industrial trash can with wheels already attached.

The design is simple, much like the Ulines. However, the Ulines have a narrow opening or sometimes a hole you can stick the trash bag through for easy removal and simple, fast application. These lack such a feature, but its not all that special as you can still perfectly fit a trash bag with ease.

The cons about the Ulines is they’re so big that you either have to leave them in one place, or have the wheels attached. This 20 gallon size is perfect for ANYTHING. It’s lightweight enough by itself, and the handles make it super easy to carry over your shoulder. It’s not so big as to when you walk, the bottom hits your hamstring/back of knee; it’s just a great size.

My review is centered around custodial so that means I use, still, the 32 gallon bags on here just because of how much trash they can hold, but also because the trash cans offer so much support. Being custodial, that means a lot of trash can ruin the overall look of these trash cans. The Ulines being so big doesn’t allow me to easily reach down to the bottom and wipe clean, but these being smaller is another plus. Any liquid drops out of the bag, I can easily give a quick swipe with my microfiber cloth and done. If not, a quick rinse with the hose works also. Another quick wipe around the outside and inside of the trash can and they’re like new.

I will update my review when and if these trash cans end up breaking/cracking from the bottom as the Ulines tend to do so, but only after irresponsible staff slam them on the ground. These trash cans, however, are not “as” sturdy in material like the Ulines, but show some serious competition. The real test is when the trash is just loaded, piled on it that it’s heavy to lift because then that’ll show if it’s really sturdy enough to handle weight and pressure. I haven’t gotten there yet, and don’t plan on purposely doing so, but some food for thought.

In the end, these trash cans are super great because they offer just as much support, space, and are inexpensive. Definitely recommend to anyone, not just custodial.

Another plus is you can purchase wheels and attach them to the bottom in case anyone is using these for the main trash can around the house. Throw on some wheels, your bag, and you can easily walk around the house, around the cemented yard, through the driveway, just about anywhere with a flat surface for an easy trash experience. Going to look into purchasing a pair of 32 gallons next, but not in white.
top_profane.title: Some very good trash cans

I purchased the set from Amazon on Sept 29th. The set I received had a date sticker of 08/30/17 so I think they are quickly moving quite a few of these sets through Amazon. I believe this set is a good value for the money as it has quite a few different files for different filing needs. Through Amazon, the set was $65.35. If purchased elsewhere, the prices were ranging from $65.35 all the way up to $146.99. If you purchased all 8 pieces direct from Nicholson Tools, it would run you about $128.38 without the storage pouch.

Things about this set to note:
1) The files DO NOT have an individual part number stamped on them. Just a generic description like "Flat bastard".
2) The set does not have a file small enough to file chainsaw chains as pictured on the Amazon listing. I would recommend a file specifically meant for chainsaw chains.
3) The pictures on Amazon are misleading. The red pouch and files say USA. I received a green pouch and South American made files.

All in all, I believe this set is a good value for the money. It arrived undamaged and all the files were in excellent condition.

Four stars for the product, would have been five but the Amazon listing is misleading showing USA products and misleading product use (sharpening a chainsaw chain).

I'll be totally honest. I've always wanted a simplehuman trash can for my kitchen, but I could never justify paying what I considered to be "big money" for a trash can. So for the last 20 years or so, my kitchen has been the home to cheap plastic Rubbermaid trash cans with a foot pedal to open and close them. Such cans are cheap, which is good. But they really aren't ideal. They get dirty very easily, the bag doesn't always stay in place, and they break. So, when I was given the opportunity to review this beautiful simplehuman trash can after 20 years of using "trashy" trash cans...well, I jumped at the chance.

The trash can arrived at my house packed very carefully: A box within a box, with lots of "air pillows" to take up any extra space. When I unpacked it, I was really surprised at how beautiful this trash can really is. Beautiful AND well-constructed! It's a little taller than the trash cans I'm used to using, but I'm sure I'll adjust to that. The brushed stainless is top-notch and I just love the simplicity of this can. The top lifts completely off to provide easy access to the inner plastic bucket that holds the bag. This makes removing a full bag and replacing it with an empty bag very simple. I also love that you can fold the top of the bag over the inner bucket so that none of the bag shows after you put the top back on. It's a very clean look.

The Touch Bar works effortlessly--use your hand, your knee, your hip, or whatever to push it. When the lid opens it's virtually silent. One thing I absolutely love: When the lid opens, it opens to slightly less than a 90-degree angle. The great thing about this is that you can push the can right up against a wall without having to worry about the lid hitting the wall when it opens. I also love that the lid stays open. Great for when you have both hands full (like when you're scraping scraps off of a cutting board and into the trash). It would be really cool if you could *close* the lid by pushing the Touch Bar, too, but shutting it by using your hand isn't that big of a deal.

For what it's worth, I haven't yet tried the specially designed-to-fit simplehuman trash bags that came with the can (size M). I am using the Costco brand of 13-gallon kitchen trash bags and they work great.

This item has changed my opinion about simplehuman trash cans. If I had it to do all over again, I would've bought one years ago. It's really nice having a trash can that isn't garbage!
top_profane.title: Nice can!!!

top_profane.text:
Don't get me wrong, this is my favorite deskside trash can in my home, and I have five desktops sized trash cans in my home. I ordered this one, and it's by far my favorite simply because of the size and how much trash it can hold in a very small amount of space while still looking professional and nice.

However, what I didn't like was that I bought it because our home is big on recycling, and I was hoping to use the side slot/compartment for paper recycling trash as it was made to be for. The compartment is so small however that there are two problems with this. The main problem is getting ANY trash bag to fit correctly on the trash can part securely while NOT covering the recycling paper compartment on the side which is only about an inch lower than the top of the trash can itself. As you can see from the photos, it is nearly impossible to get a bag to fit around it, so you basically just have to cover up the paper slot if you want your bag to be fitting securely in the trash bin.

The second problem is that the paper slot/compartment is far too small, not very much more than a day's mail can fit in it, and that's if you can even get to the compartment through the liner or around the liner. So I found using the recycling compartment on it was too much trouble than it was worth and have just given up using it and covering it with my trash liners. Finally, the compartment makes it harder to clean the trash can thoroughly.

However, this is a VERY nice-looking and professional trash bin, and it is made of heavier, very thick plastic, so it is extremely durable and as I mentioned, holds a lot of trash. So, overall I like it, but it's not worth the extra cost for the recycling compartment because it wasn't designed for realistic everyday use, makes it hard to line it, and makes it more difficult to clean. Thus, I am giving it four out of five stars. If it were just a trash bin without the paper slot on the side, or with a larger, perhaps removable slot, I would have given it a five out of five.
top_profane.title: Awesomely Huge Trash Can, But Recycling Compartment Pretty Useless

top_profane.text:
Update to my original review as of 12/02/2013:
SimpleHuman's replacement came quick and their customer service is awesome (even got a comment follow up a few months later on Amazon). I have upgraded my rating from 2-star to 5-star. The replacement works like a charm and I l-o-v-e all of SimpleHuman's products. Convenient and great that you can open with your hip when you have your hands full. By the way, I already own the butterfly-style trashcan by SimpleHuman as well as several of their sensor soap pumps. Not cheap but good quality and good looking modern products!

---------------------------

Upsides:

1) Absolutely a beautiful, impressive, sleek, stainless steel rectangular hand /knee Touch-Bar canister. Not cheap looking by a long shot.
2) Well built and sturdy.
3) I could never image calling this beautiful piece a trash can. This brings trash cans clearly up a several notches to the level of a canister. No more unsightly trash cans in your kitchens; no, now you're now the proud owner of a "canister". J
4) The flush, straight back of the canister allows placing it directly against a wall with the lid fully opened. No need any longer like other trash cans types which need to be located several inches away from a wall to allow the lid to be fully opened. Problem solved.
5) Take note that this canister is 36" tall when the lid is in the fully opened position, and 28" tall when in the fully closed position.
6) There is a plastic liner inside the canister with you place your trash bags into. This liner easily comes out allowing you to simply hose/wash the interior resulting in a fresh and sparkling clean canister.
7) I Love This Canister. If my loved one and I ever become divorced, I'm taking this canister.

Downsides:
1) .36 cents per Simplehuman customized perfect fit trash bags, per Simplehuman online website pricing, OUCH!!
2) We have tried a few other brand trash bags (13 gallon size, this canister is listed as 10.6 gallon) and found that we really had to stretch these bags just to fit over the canister lip to hold the bag inside. The problem with this is the fact now there is so much tension placed on the stretched bags that I fear that the trash bag could easily tear when trying to stuff the bag full (OK we all tend to "over stuff" our trash bags). While speaking with a engineer friend who happened to be visiting our home and we were proudly showing off our new canister and explaining our trash bag puzzling dilemma. It was found out to be that most trash bags are designed with an oval opening in mind not a rectangular shape. Hmmm!! With this problem solved it opened up another train of thought, this will almost always force the consumer (you) to use more bags ($) or the Simplehuman customized perfect fit bags at 36 cents per each, it also increases the trips to the curbs on trash day and we must use a gentler, caring approach not to "over stuff" the bags. We will continue to experiment with other bags seeking the "perfect" fit; maybe someone has a little clue or secret that they would love to share to overcome this.
3) On our canister we have just received from the shipper, the lid won't always close with a gentle touch without having to hold the lid down and activating numerous times the Touch-Bar (opening lever bar) several times so that the lid will "catch" and remain closed. Oh and our friend the engineer, that's right, you guessed it, he was lucky enough to be able to simple close and latch the lids every time, while the others couldn't. Brrrr.
3) Our canister lid has on numerous occasions open up on its own even after having made sure the lid was closed securely and no one is nearby the maybe accidently touching the canister.

Conclusion:
OK, I know what you're thinking; yes I seem to have gotten a slightly defective canister. I'm quite certain that after contacting the Simplehuman Corp. customer service department that they will rectify or replace my unit with ease. Simplehuman Corp. has been an outstandingly great company and continues to maintain their stellar reputation, that's exactly why we purchase Simplehuman products.

I really do like this canister so much that I'm willing to compromise and continue searching for just the right trash bag without the 36 cent cost (any ideas anyone). The price of this canister ($139.99) might seem hefty on the upfront costs, but once you place this in your kitchens the naysayers with cross over and applaud your taste.

I will update this product review after we get the warranty replacement from SimpleHuman.
top_profane.title: I wanted to like it but it doesn't stay closed. Waiting for warranty replacement...

top_profane.text: I really like the electronic eye feature.  I have mobility problems such that a trash can with a "step on the pedal to lift the lid" mechanism doesn't work for me.  Two other issues that this trash can resolved for me:  1) My cat would tug on overhanging trash bags to pull my other trash cans over. 2) My daughter stops by only every 2 weeks or so to take my trash out for me. This trash can resolved these issues because it is extra large and it works with trash bags kept entirely inside. It requires four "D" batteries to work. It has an on/off button, so I set it for "off" when I am not in the kitchen just in case my cat tries jumping on it, etc. It comes with swivel wheels to attach to the bottom, so it is easily moved although it is large.  It is extremely attractive for a trash can, so it looks just fine in my kitchen although it is larger than most trash cans folks keep in their kitchens.  When received, I only had to attach the wheels to the bottom of the can and install the batteries and this can was ready to go!  It is working great so far.  I was a bit concerned about some reviews that said the can quit working after about a year.  I noticed, though, that one kind reviewer posted instructions on how to reset the opening mechanism and I am relying on his information.  Just to make sure everyone knows how to reset this item, here's how once again per that kind reviewer:  "turn the mechanism off; take out batteries and leave them out for six hours; replace batteries; then turn it back on."  The total cost with shipping, etc. was $206.  When I think about the fact that I spent over $200 for a trash can, I feel a little foolish except when I think about how well this particular trash can resolved issues for me that no others could.  Also, other brands of this size are nearly as costly, but don't have the electronic eye feature. So, all in all, I'm pleased with this trash can and looking forward to many years of not having to clean up trash messes created by my cat!
top_profane.title: Great for "gimpy" folks like me!

Couldn't find a step trash can this large locally, so bought this model online. The shipping dept put the trash can in a box and then the box inside another box for extra care and no damage during shipping. When the huge box arrived I followed the instructions to assemble the trash can and had a trash bag in the thing within a few minutes. The real treat is on the inside where the walls are smooth plastic and nothing snags the trash bag when trying to remove it to take it outside to the garbage bin. Also, the larger size means it can hold 30-32 gallon trash bags, which equates to less trips out to the garbage bin. I chose the black color over tan because food stains and grease spills won't show up as easy on the black plastic vs a lighter tan model.

I wouldn't use this as an outside trash can though, because squirrels and racoons like to sharpen their teeth on the plastic in the fall and you will end up with a bunch of chew marks and holes in your trash can. Only use galvanized metal trash cans if you don't want the night critters chewing a hole in your trash can, tearing up your trash bags, and then scattering food debris all over your back yard.

top_profane.text:
I've owned this product for over a year, and after trying to fix the thing myself (I'm an electrical engineer), I realize why this and other shoddy Chinese manufacturer electronics are considered "crap".

-Interface-
The interface is terribly unresponsive using the remote. I find I have to press the same button two or three times to get the menu to change. The reason: the component that you point the remote at is a piece of crap. Its a low quality transistor receiver that was poorly assembled, and it was also showing signs of rust. Now rust inside your electronics: first sign of low quality equipment. Technical mumbo-jumbo aside, it is not a very pretty interface compared to other multimedia players I've seen. A bland black background with poor font selection. The people who wrote/designed the menu interface were clearly the kind of people who just do a job just to get things done, don't to make it better

-Unit itself-
It's small and light weight, a pro if you plan on bringing it around to places. But the placement of the cable ports is a poor design decision. If you're not using HDMI and have to use component or composite connection, you might find that you have to plug the audio/visual wires on two sides of the unit (on the back and right side of the unit). Very ugly and very unfortunate if you put this thing in between some stuff like a game console or against a wall. The unit may be like 6 inches wide, but you'll need like at least 1 foot of clearance around the sides of the unit. It's even more ugly if you stand it up.

-Remote-
Looks cheaper than the universal remotes you find at the dollar store. The buttons are too small and too tightly packed together. And I have medium sized hands for a guy. As I said before, you have to press the same button two or three times to get it to change menu or pause/play/forward/rewind. Buttons are even a little hard to press too.

-My personal experience-
At first it was good, despite the design flaws. it worked as it should, played all sorts of media formats. However I found the more I used it, the more I got annoyed by it. I hated the annoying clicking sound when you press a button to select an item or enter a menu. While playing a video, I found the pause/play/seek bar obtrusive and blocked about 10% of the video. Then the unit broke after a year. I took it apart to find it used crappy, destined to fail capacitors. Being an engineer, I tried to replace them. And in the middle of repairing it, the crappy capacitor pin got stuck inside, making it nearly impossible to replace that capacitor. Thats how I knew that these things were pieces of crap and were no longer worth my time repairing.

-Overall-
If you really just need a quick and cheap multimedia player, this is not a bad product. HOWEVER if you are looking for something that is reliable and has a chance of lasting more than 2 years, I suggest you look at another brand. It uses cheap and unreliable components that are more likely to fail than a blind man taking his driver's license test.
top_profane.title: Unreliable Chinese Garbage Multimedia Unit

top_profane.text:
The iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can, made in China, measures 8.9 x 6.4 x 10.3 inches which is smaller than two gallons of milk placed side by side. The trash can with its thick bottom and side which seem to get thinner towards the top weighs 1.8 pounds.

I could use a couple small trash cans for the bathrooms. The iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can seems too small for typical business use, but its compact size seems perfect for squeezing in between a cabinet and toilet. No thought was given to the trash can’s appearance, although the trash can is functional.

I didn’t test the trash can’s fire resistance, however fiberglass is an inorganic fire resistant compound. Fiberglass items that burn tend to be manufactured with burnable epoxies. It shouldn’t be that difficult to create a fire-resistant fiberglass trash can simply by selecting the right ingredients to blend the fiberglass with.

The iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can is compact and fire-resistant. While the durable trash can may be aesthetically displeasing, it will perform its task well. If you’re looking for a small trash can and fire resistance is a concern, the iLIVING commercial fiberglass fire-resistant 7 quart rectangular trash can will get the job done.

PROS:
Fire resistant
Compact size perfect for tight spaces
Durable design

CONS:
Basic appearance
top_profane.title: Basic: An iLIVING Commercial Fiberglass Fire-Resistant 7 Quart Rectangular Trash Can Review

Again, I apologize to all for the review I posted first. I had just gotten the printer and had not run into their foul tech support. I did NOT know any better! Boy, I DO know better now! If you buy this printer, PLEASE! DO YOUR RESEARCH! Find out about the problems with support BEFORE YOU BUY. Believe me, there are a bazillion complaints about XYZ support online. All you have to do is look for it.

------- My original review is below here --------

Well... I read all the reviews here before buying. A lot of the issues people complain of are idiocy issues apparently. Blasted thing weighs a frickin TON! Man, I wasn't expecting THAT much weight! The box looked reasonably okay. I unpacked it - me and my 10 year old son. We, being GUYS, ignored the directions - of course! And you know what? We got it unpacked and set up anyway! I dunno. So far it seems idiot proof...

It took me a good 5 minutes to unpack. It took a while to feed the filament. It took multiple attempts to load it - apparently you just keep hitting retry until it extrudes, and it is visibly obvious when it does extrude. After you hit okay to show it loaded, it then cleans the nozzle automatically. Nice! Took maybe 8 minutes to feed the filament. Probably 2 minutes from now on since I know what to expect...

And then... I ran through the setup steps. I was a good boy. I folowed the manual. I'm sure it took over 15 minutes to get this far. Maybe 16 minutes?

And then... Magic! I am printing the first sample - the star vase.

Everything is working right. The vase is half finished. It looks AMAZING! Far out, man... It does what it says it will do! Imagine that!

The light does go off in the print chamber but it is EASY to turn on again! I've spent 40 minutes watching in fascination. It says 45 minutes to go. Looking good!

I bought the blue painter's tape sheet. Didn't want to mess with the glue stick after reading people's comments. The tape sheet is working great. And now I see why. If you don't have something down there for the plastic to stickTO it just isn't going to stick, period. The glass is far too slick.

The blue tape sheet is doing a great job so far. And so is the printer! I have no doubt I'll have more to say later, but initial impression and initial experience are both rather great! Which is amazing, because from the condition of the shipping container I expected it to be dead. There were bracing materials inside the printer that were BROKEN! My heart sank when I saw that. The big red strap that was supposed to hold the carriage immobile was SNAPPED IN TWO! And yet... It SEEMS to be working perfectly! Kudos on a study machine that obviously took a really bad whack but which seems to be performing impeccably!

I had it in my paws on time, and it is working quite well. I'd say this is a winner.

Would I reccomend it? You betcha! I'm quite happy with it! 5 stars is what it deserves at this point. Cheers!
-----------------------------------------
2015/01/26 Oh, what a LIE! I was IGNORANT! I'm STILL happy with the HARDWARE but I absolutely DO NOT RECCOMEND XYZ Ithe company) to ANYONE!

top_rant.text:
The idea is good, the execution is a disaster.
This was originnaly created to be a replacement for a classic intercom system, but with video and access from another home or while on the road. It falls short even on that!

The product was marketed at a premium price with the implicit understanding they would not charge you a monthly fee (at least one would think, everyone who ever had an intercom system knows it's a wired or radio item like a walkie talkie, would you ever pay a monthly amount for a walkie talkie?, but more on that later on).

With a classic intercom you:

1) Only have audio, no video (unless you have a video enabled one, very rare and usually expensive)
2) Can't access from outside the home
3) Can't make video calls between smartphone and the devices in different houses over the internet
4) Are relegated to a fixed location to use it unless you bought a cordless (radio) one with a battery (think wired landline vs cordless landline)

With Nucleus:
1) You have audio + video, yes!
2) You can access it over the internet from outside your home
3) Can call with your internet connected smarthphone and between devices in different houses over the internet
4) Are relegated to a fixed location to use the device because they didn't think they could add a small replaceable battery to give you an option to move while using it (big fail over $5 more in manufacturing costs increase if you ask me).

But wait, this is, after all, a customized tablet-like device so it should be able to do everything a tablet (or at least an echo show, google home etc.) does, so why not??

I see the point in keeping it partially closed for simplicity (you want it for be simple for elderly people) and for it to be more stable than a tablet (that could be infected and slowed by malware etc.) but i don't see the point in not giving you the chance to do little more (via a hidden, "advanced" menu).
Think if you could load youtube, disney+ or other tv apps/platforms, think if you could use alexa or google home (in fairness you have limited support for Alexa, but the mic is not sensitive at all!).
The had added support for ring and backed out (1st why just ring and not universal rstp access to your lan connected doorbells and security cameras?). Why doesn't let you to use you security camera apps??
They didn't have to open you to the play store or fire store or whatever, but giving you the chance to load media players (vlc anyone?), security cameras/doorbells/baby monitors and home automation (smartthings et similia) apps from the major players would have been a real game changer!

In any case, they miserably failed on the expanded potential I just described that would have costed them very little to implement for you and $0 months in recurring expenses for them but would have expanded greatly the usefulness and widespread adoption of the device (1000× fold and more the numbers of devices hey sold so far).

A pity. Regardless, after that much money, one would expect AT THE BARE MINIMUM is a replacement for a classic audio intercom to use inside the walls of you home.

Nucleus fails miserably at that too, let me tell you why:

1) If you don't have internet you cannot use the device at all!!! Not even inside your local network over lan or wifi!!
In fairness, if you had the devices connected to the internet when you first installed and configured them AND they are under the same network with internet access the moment you power them ON as long as you don't power them OFF even if the network drops or loses the internet they can call each other like in the same home/local network p2p like a classic intercom. But if one loses power (remember, no battery) for any reason (you can use a ups behind each one of them but even that is just a partial solution) then you lost that one until internet is back on and they are authorized again by their server to work inside your lan!
They clearly don't need a server to work, so why don't they communicate one another on the same lan if they first don't connect with Nucleus's Server every single time they power on? No reason except this way they can brick your device anytime they want and keep you hostage or call it a service where there is no service provided!

I am very sad to say that this is what is happening!
After charging an arm and a leg for the device people who supported and believed them, giving them a poorly exectuted product and limiting (without advantage for them but at a great disavantage for you) what this device can do and load (remember no video doorbell, no video player, no home automation apps, no biggest players on the market, no your 2 or 3 favorite apps) they have now decided to brick them for good!!!

This means:
1) No tablet like features or app of any kind (never had anyway , such a missed opportunity)
2) NO MORE you can call with your internet connected smarthphone and between devices in different houses over the internet!!
3) NO MORE you can access it over the internet from outside your home!!!
4 ) NO MORE you have video AND NO MORE you have audio even from INSIDE you home, room to room.
5) NO MORE it works even as LOCAL AUDIO ONLY INTERCOM

You read right, your VERY expensive Nucleus Intercom NO more works even as DUMB audio only (let alone video) intercom inside your home / local network!!!!
Wait, but my 30 years classic audio intercom still works, why the Nucleus does not????

Well, your classic intercom is going to work (and you can fix it if it breaks) until your house is not demolished or lost in a fire and that is going to cost you the cost of the repairs, if needed. It will never require a subscription, you can use the hardware you paid for until it burns.

The Nucleus as of today (oct 15th 2020) it's still working but that is going to change in a few days and like I said no more anything, UNLESS you start paying a monthly subscription of $5 month ($1800 in 30 years) for each device!!!
People who use it as intercom have 4 or more of them , so think about a whopping $9000 in subscriptions for 5 devices to reach the 30 years lifetime of your old audio only intercom!!

This service was supposed to be like Tivo lifetime (you pay a lot now and until the device works you don't pay a subscription, and not even, because tivo has a cost to cover to give you the guide, nucleus only cost is artificial and avoudable on their part, you don't need any service from theyr server from a tech standpoint for it to work).
But people in charge of Nucleus were stupid enough to:

1) Have Amazon rip them off without assuring some patents for themselves (google if you like)
2) Limiting you from loading the major videoplayers/home automation/doorbell /etc apps to make the device more versatile for NO real reason but with the dare consequence of very limited adoption by the general public!!
3) Instead of improving - at very little cost to them by just giving you more things it can do with capacity to load 3rd party apps /functions - on their original device to have more people buy it (and later on selling you new devices with bigger screens, better mike and so on) they discontinued it and decided to switch to the nursing home market!!!
Their new device (Nucleus care) is the same hardwarewise but with a different software to entice nursing homes with a monthly subscription service. Even this time they failed!!! They aren't selling enough subscriptions or devices (who tought focusing in an even smaller niche was a good idea is a genius [instead of expanding the original device abilities for the general public with the moves I said - and selling the subscription "medical" services to the nursing homes as an add-on] ).
Consequence is now they are trying to sell the "medical" subscription device to the general public (who doesn't byte or care for that, so they don't know how to pay the bills, or don't they???).
Remember the original user base, the ones left with a very expensive crippled device, never expanded and for which they even dropped the little limited expansions like ring, the ones that cost them $0.10 each every 50 years in server costs to authorize (again no tech reason) their nucleus connect p2p each other???
Nucleus decided these customers can pay the bills!!!
While Nucleus has his personel and development and monthly current expenses towards Nucleus care, decides that to foot the bill has to be the intercom customer!!!

I wish they had the decency to unlock the device in order for you to be able to use it as an intercom inside your home at least or load your own apps since you paid for it.
They are spending (very little) server money to be able to prevent your devices to work unless their server was pinged in the process just for them to be in control!!!
These are p2p devices they do everything, there is no service running on their servers except the ones to authorize the devices to go ahead and do what they are already capable of doing!!!!
They don't need to spend money to keep servers on they end for our nucleus to keep running and working (110% certified on nucleus to nucleus intercom call on same network) and we would not need them if they just unlocked our nucleus not to ask permission to start working to them!!

How would you feel if you bought a car and after a couple of years they told you:
Your car has everything to work and brings you around but we built it so that it won't move unless our company sends an authorization to start over the internet...we sent this authorization for free so far (well you had to pay for the internet but that's another story), but from now on we want a monthly subscrition fee to send this authorization. Would people pay or would people file a class action and have the manufacturer remove this silly, artificial requirement for the car to start?

Don't give a penny to these thieves! Let them fail, they are leaving us with very expensive coasters!!!

top_rant.text:
Do I need to say it again? This is a horribly designed printer from a horrible company.

Print quality:
While this is a kit printer, and while most printers have the ability to produce a high quality print, THIS PRINTER CANNOT. You may see a couple people with high-quality prints, but trust me, that is not repeatable without heavy modification to the printer. And I'm talking HEAVY. Modifications. Why? The basalt or MDF print bed is not flat, it droops down towards the center. Your prints will lift off the bed due to poor adhesion on small prints, or there will be heavy Z-Axis banding (looks like waves on the side of the print). You have a choice of a failed print or a crappy print, especially if the print covers a large area. And since this printer does not come with endstops, you're more likely to get a failed print anyways.

Reliability:
This thing is THE FARTHEST THING FROM RELIABLE. I've had this printer for 4 months now. 2 of those months were spend waiting for replacement parts to ship, because this thing has broken down more times than I can count. In fact, the reason why I've finally decided to write a review is because the horrible design of the printer has chewed up one of my more expensive modifications to the printer. The MDF frame is weak, and screws will strip the wood easily. The belts are horribly positioned on the X and Y axis. There are so many reasons why this printer is unreliable, but these are the big ones, aside from the one I outlined in the Print Quality section.

Support:
This is a kit printer, which means quality, speedy support is a must. Whoops. 2 week response time. Guess what? If you don't order from Amazon, they'll make that even longer, so as not to anger the reviewers. Whoops. 1 month+ response time. Oh, and good luck getting anything out of them at all, the company seems to be dead anyways by now. Hmm. I wonder why.

Ease of use:
I'm sure you can guess this one. Ease of use is obviously second to none. I will say that the build guide wasn't horrible, most of the directions made sense, except for the part where they left out the instructions for my specific printer. It was fun, trying to figure that one out. At this point I'm not sure if I'm being sarcastic or not.

Value:
This is one of the cheaper printers on the market, but there are better alternatives. There are other 200 USD printers that I'll bet can print better than this thing. And even if there weren't, I still wish I'd bought a different printer. I've spent more time and money trying to fix this thing than I would've spent on, like, ANY. OTHER. PRINTER. (Except maybe Makerbot.) I've spent maybe 250USD+ (on the ThreeUp w/ Heated Bed, a 470 USD printer) trying to get this thing to work properly. I wish I'd just bought a more expensive printer. I'll have a short recommendation section below.

Community:
This printer's one saving grace. The buyers of Q3D's printers are many, and some have gone on to design their own printers, despite being quite new to the 3D printing scene. This is because Q3D has created such a s*** printer that some members of the community were so determined to get their money's worth and make the printer not crappy, because this printer is so crappy that it will force you to learn every intricacy of FFF printers, because I'm stuck with a 600 USD printer made of REGRET. Please. PLEASE. Do not take this as an invitation to buy this printer, or buy any printer less than about 500 USD without doing a lot of research. And no, those positive amazon reviews do not count.

Conclusion: Buy another printer. Don't buy any printer less than 500ish USD without asking some enthusiasts about the printer.

-------

Recommendations:
Since you're most likely a first-time buyer, I won't highlight the more advanced printers.

600-750 USD: Printrbot Simple, available as a kit or assembled. If anything, this will far surpass Q3D's advertised "Makerbot quality." It's in line with many higher-priced printers, despite being small. For 400 USD, Printrbot also offers a much smaller printer.

500ish USD: Wanhao Duplicator I3: A decent printer, based on the Prusa I3 design. Large community.

400ish? : DagomaDiscovery 200 A little-known printer. Lack of community means this is a massive gamble, but other than that, it can't be worse than Q3D's stuff.

270 USD: Folger Tech 2020 Prusa i3: Kinda like the Wanhao printer, but cheaper. You will need to get your own glass for the build plate, and you're likely to be making some expensive upgrades, but this is the printer I wish I'd started out with. The frame is made out of aluminum and the build area is (slightly) larger than that of the ThreeUp. It even comes with a heated bed! If you want to be forced to learn how 3D printers work, but don't want the steaming pile of ^&*% that I've had to deal with, buy this. Good luck!

Want to keep looking on your own? Here are some tips.

Stay away from: Makerbot, DaVinci, and Q3D (duh). The Makerbot has a lot of bad reputation hidden behind the well-known name for many, many reasons. Davinci's printers have proprietary filament refills.

Delta printers are not beginner-friendly, unless you are an engineer who likes a challenge. Google to find out what a delta looks like.

If you have time and want to learn from your printer, look for "Open source" printers. If the hardware is open source, it means you can easily get the 3d files of printer parts, allowing you to design custom modifications for your printer easier. If the software is open source, it means you're getting a very flexible printer that will work with many different softwares. Software can make a huge difference in reliability and print quality and ease of use.

Heated Beds make printing almost *everything* easier. If you're a beginner, don't try to print without one.

Bowden printers have many advantages and disadvantages, unless you've done a massive amount of research or are buying a very reputable printer, I do not recommend buying a bowden printer the first time.

Layer resolution is a marketing gimmick. Most printers can print to 20 microns (0.02 mm). It's simply that doing so is not always practical, not always reliable, not very easy, and certainly not very fast. I'd be suspicious if the printer's advertised layer resolution is lower than 40 microns, or higher than 200 microns. Overall, there's no mechanical reason why a printer cannot print less than 200 microns. Other than that, ignore the layer resolution when comparing printers.

Want a higher end printer? Here are some reputable names: Ultimaker, Makergear, LulzBot. I'd pick LulzBot if I weren't worried about cash. These are pre-assembled printers.

Be prepared to spend *some* time on any printer less than 1k USD.

Check with your local makerspace to see what they recommend. They'll hate me for saying this, but if they recommend it, they'll probably be able to help you with it. This doesn't mean you shouldn't still do some research on their suggestions though.

------

Need some more help finding a printer?
The RepRap IRC is sure to be full of friendly people willing to help you out. [...]IRC Introduce yourself! Ask questions! Just be a bit patient.

The RepRap wiki has a lot of information about 3D printers, though it may not always be noob-friendly, it's incredibly useful and an indispensable tool for many.

The reddit community will help you out as long as you've read the rules and done some research beforehand. [...] They also have a small chart of well-known printers, though not all of them are noob-friendly or cheap. [...]
Here's another chart of 3D printers from 3D hubs. Remember, do your research. [...]
------

Good luck!
