top_rant.text:
I never thought I would love a vacuum cleaner more than my Shark Navigator. There's nothing like a clean dust free house, and before my Shark, no Hoover, Eureka, Dyson or any other brand I owned could hold a candle to the Shark's superior cleaning power, maneuverability, washable filters and light weight. Shark was the best 5 Star vacuum I ever owned until now. This revolutionary new Dyson DC50 is not anything like the Dyson I used to own. The Dyson I owned years ago was so heavy, I couldn't wait to replace it with a lighter one- which I did when I found my Shark Navigator. Now, my Shark is the heavy vacuum- which I didn't know until using this unbelievably lightweight DC50.

When I compare vacuum cleaners, I have a test I like to do. I let my rugs and hard floors get filthy for over a week. Then I take one room and vacuum it with my old vacuum cleaner, which is currently my Shark. I then follow up with the new vacuum cleaner which is my Dyson DC50. My Shark cleaned it perfectly, or what I thought was perfectly, until I followed up with another cleaning with the Dyson DC50. The Dyson DC50 picked up another cup of dirt and dust- that's 8 ounces of more dirt and dust that my Shark left behind! I couldn't believe it- my 5 Star Shark being outdone by a vacuum half it's weight and size! I then vacuum another room in the opposite manner. I vacuum first with the new Dyson DC50, then follow up with my favorite Shark. The Shark actually picked up a teaspoon of fluff- it looked like carpet fibers more than dust. Dyson picked up everything again, with the exception of a teaspoon of carpet fibers.

Next, some of my family and friends wanted to do my room test with my new Dyson DC50, as they couldn't wait to actually see this new vacuum in action. The results were all the same- the Dyson DC50 picked up at least a cup or more of dirt and dust on every carpet or floor. It didn't matter which 5 star brand Dyson went up against- Dyson's DC50 left every other top name vacuum in the dust. I then wanted to know how this is possible, that a tiny vacuum could outperform all these other top brands, including my favorite Shark, so I took a close look at this revolutionary new Dyson DC50. What I found is that this small barely over 11 pound vacuum is made of pure lightweight power. There are 19 light weight inner cyclones- 19- that's double the suction power of any other vacuum!!! Dyson put 2 tiers instead of their usual one tier of their powerful radial cyclones on this DC50. The powerul, yet light weight motor is in the ball to save weight and space. The long telescoping wand and hose is inside the handle! Talk about your well designed machine- everything is compact, neatly arranged with unheard of cyclonic power in a minimal space! Dyson made this DC50 compact in it's cord as well. The cord is a little over 25 feet long, where my Shark has a 30 foot cord. I honestly find 25 feet of cord long enough for every room in my house, as I have outlets on every wall, and I also find myself not tripping over a longer cord, which I always did with my Shark.

When you first open the box, you will see the vacuum, handle with telescoping wand inside, brush head, holder for attachments, 3 attachments and of course the directions and booklets. I needed no directions, as you carefully lay the vacuum down on it's back to attach the brush head. Stand it up and attach the handle with wand to the hose and then onto the vacuum. When you want to vacuum with the wand or tube, you just flip open the top lid on the handle and push up on the arrow on the red wand top to slide the wand off. Then, if you only want the tube, you can press the red button to release the wand from the tube. After attaching the brush head and telescoping wand inside the handle, you lay the vacuum face down and attach the holder for the two attachments at the bottom back. Only 3 steps and you are ready to vacuum.

There are 2 buttons- the power ON/OFF and the Brush Off button. You can use the Brush Off Button for hard floors that you don't want scratched with brush bristles, but also for carpets. Should you have a new carpet that is shedding too much or an old expensive hand woven one or delicate one that you don't want bristles on, you can vacuum with no bristles and it just lifts the dirt as if the bristles are on- it's suction is that powerful with those 19 cyclones! The vacuum always resets itself to brush on when you are finished, so remember to set it to brush off, whenever you need to vacuum without the bristles. You will especially love the bristles off for heavy long pet hair- although no matter how long or heavy the pet hair, these bristles never get tangled. I know, as my neighbor's border collie's hair which is super long and thick did nothing to the bristles! It sucked up every inch of hair as if the bristles were off and not moving. Families with animal members will absolutely LOVE this vacuum, which is powerfully made for vacuuming heavy hair! Besides the option of powerful cleaning with or without bristles, this brush head self adjusts to the height of your carpet or hard floors! No wonder it doesn't miss a piece of dust- it is self adjusting in height to the floor! I've never owned a vacuum this smart and efficient!

This small 11 inch wide brush head fits in the narrowest of spaces as the rotating ball lets the head turn on a dime. This Dyson turn on a dime ball is made of ABS and polycarbonate- the same material used in riot shields and crash helmets! Talk about strong plastic. Should you need a smaller brush head, Dyson has included a tiny 5 inch by 3 inch brush head with rotating bristles and it also has the self adjusting height! You can attach this tiny mini brush head to the wand and get in any teensy spaces! There are 2 other attachments as well, a stair attachment tool and a combination attachment tool that can telescope up from a skinny suction end to a wider brush end. These tool attachments are all I will ever need to reach my 10 foot ceilings all the way down to under my bed and dressers and behind my refrigerator and appliances. The telescoping wand extends a full 36 inches long- that's twice the length of my Shark wand of 18 inches! Both my Shark and this Dyson DC50 will sit at the bottom of my stairs and extend a full 8 steps up with their tubes and wands while vacuuming- with one major exception. When I pick up the vacuum to move up a few steps to finish the stairs, I need 2 hands to lift the Shark. I need ONE FINGER to lift the Dyson DC50!

I am over 50 years old and can lift this vacuum with ONE FINGER! I have never been able to lift and carry any vacuum cleaner in my life with one finger until now. This Dyson DC50 is truly revolutionary. The plug is super soft and comfortable to pull in and out of the socket. The handle telescopes down when storing it, to a mere 30 1/2 inches tall. My Shark is 46 1/2 inches tall! The 2 lifetime filters are washable with just a water rinse, shake and air dry overnight, and the filters are HEPA filtration. One filter is in the ball, the other in the dirt container. The dirt container snaps out and with the push of a stick it opens the bottom and releases the dirt, then you just snap back on the bottom, and put it back on the vacuum. The dirt container lets you know when to empty the dirt with it's easily visible MAX lines. There is a 5 year warranty, as with all superb vacuums. Now for more fabulous news, this Dyson DC50 leaves NO STRIPING on your carpet! Every vacuum I have ever owned left what I call stripe runway marks on my carpet. This vacuum leaves no striped runways!

I don't know how Dyson did it, but they created my first 10 STAR vacuum with this DC50 Animal Compact Upright Vacuum Cleaner. I can lift it with one finger, it has 19 cyclones in 2 layers for tremendous pure power you can find no where else, the thickest hair never tangles, it has a self adjusting brush head, you can use it without the bristles, the ball whips around corners, it fits in a closet space 30 1/2 inches high, by 15 inches deep and 11 inches wide, it has 2 lifetime washable Hepa filters, 3 tools you can use from your ceilings to under your bed, it has a 5 year warranty, no stripe runway marks on your carpet and it picks up more dirt after I vacuum with my favorite 5 star vacuum cleaner. You never have to buy bags, belts or filters- that alone saves a small fortune. Dyson has truly revolutionized vacuuming with their DC50 Animal Compact Upright and you will love the pure power and lightest weight that cleans up beautifully after your family and furry family members both!
top_rant.title: Dyson DC50 Animal- TWICE the Power, HALF the Size, 5 Year Warranty

top_rant.text:
[[VIDEOID:ad4995a435d308ec49f338c629779c45]] I've only had this for 4 days so I'll post an update as I get more use out of it.

I read the reviews here and found that many of the negative reviews are not accurate, at least not in terms of what I've experienced. In general this is a great machine that works really well with all the dual extrusion and IDEX capabilities. It probably isn't for a beginner but someone that is willing to spend time working thru setup and calibration as this is a critical part of making this machine work correctly.

Unfortunately the documentation isn't necessarily up to date, many of the documents reflect the machine before the upgraded extruders so determining the steps (x, y, z, e) has to be done manually, not a problem but it isn't - build it, boot it and start printing dual extrusions - you'll need to work thru the calibration steps (pretty easy to do).

Pros:
1. Capable IDEX machine
2. Prints multiple materials, multiple colors
3. Excellent support (if used thru Amazon)
4. Well made and easy to put together
5. Nice UI that is pretty intuitive and a responsive touchscreen
6. Extruder 1 to 240c, Extruder 2 to 300c, Bed to 110c
7. Direct extrusion (no Bowden BS)

Cons:
1. Cable management (very minor to me and can be augmented using 3D printed parts)
2. Power supply fan is loud but printing operation is quiet (2209 drivers)
3. Takes some time to calibrate (~ 2 hours)
4. No purge buckets (again, easily printed)
5. Filament sensors not mounted (doesn't cause a problem as far as I can tell) but can easily be fixed
6. Proprietary firmware, this isn't problem (yet) but may be in the future

The cons are very minor and most can be fixed by printing various parts located on Thingiverse (wire guides, filament guide, sensor holder, etc). The biggest problem for me is the loud power supply fan, I'll look at replacing it with something quiet. The Pros outweigh the Cons by a lot, I've used many dual print systems (this is my first IDEX) but this one is more functional and it produces really nice prints in many different materials (printed TPU, CF, PLA, ABS, HIPS, PetG). I use it to print parts for various machine prototypes I build so aesthetics isn't my highest priority, instead I care about material selection and accuracy which it does really well (my 20x20x20 block prints at 20.1x19.9x20.0!). While it isn't the fastest machine it does support duplicate and mirror printing with a huge build volume to print multiple copies which increases the productivity considerably.

I've read that support is non-existent. Definitely not my experience at all, Tenlog has been outstanding in terms of support but I used Amazon to initiate the request which was then passed on to Tenlog. While we have to use the Amazon UI to get the support Tenlog was more responsive than almost any other vendor I've dealt with (3dp or not)! I had a problem with the BMG (E2) extruder where the adjustment wheel had been stripped, contacted Amazon->Tenlog and they shipped a new one from China (takes about 6-8 days for shipping to PA from China). In the interim I was able to make the BMG extruder work by simply super gluing the adjustment wheel to the bolt - works fine now.

The BS glass plate is really nice but I prefer spring steel covered with PEI, I got one from Amazon but haven't used it yet because I haven't had any problems with first layer adhesion. I do use a couple different bed glue types for my other machines but haven't yet had to use it here yet. Most of prints are generally small to I haven't pushed the envelope that much but with 2 options for the build plate and the available adhesives I don't expect a problem when I print bigger models using ABS or HIPS. I have Nylon just haven't printed with it yet.

I highly recommend this machine if you are looking for dual extrusion, wide range of material options and a large print volume. So far (its been 4 days but its been printing pretty much non-stop) I really am impressed, it represents a great value!

Now, all that said, the thing could blow up tomorrow and I'd be pissed but if it does I'll post an update explaining what happened otherwise I'll update this review again one or two times as I use it more and continue to print with different materials.

Summary:
Get the machine if you are willing to put in the time calibrating it (not hard just takes some time). Given the functionality of this machine and the associated price ($699 with $100 off so $599) I wouldn't even consider a single extrusion machine again. Even if you are brand new to 3d printing but willing to make an effort to learn this machine is a winner!

UPDATE: February 13, 2022
I've now had this printer about a month and my opinion of it is higher then when I did the original review. It's a great machine, I've had no problems with it - it just works. The Company sent me a new extruder when I complained that the adjustment knob was broken on the original (I fixed it and have been using it ever since but now have a backup of Extruder 2). I definitely recommend this printer especially if it isn't your first machine (and even then I'd strongly consider it). This printer has been running almost nonstop for a month and the only failures I've had were created by my modeling - not the printer. I've added a few additional pictures of prints completed but I've printed dozens of parts at this point. The duplication mode is outstanding I use it all the time - twice as many parts for half the time! Duplication and Mirror mode allow this machine to compete with my FLSun Super Racer in terms of part production, even though the FLSun is twice as fast. I've successfully printed TPU, PLA, ABS, ASA, PetG and HIPS. Have yet to print the infused materials but that's coming.

I did replace the bed with the PEI coated spring steel bed, it works better than the glass for some materials but for PLA the glass works beautifully. I printed purge buckets (33 minute print) and filament sensor holders (47 minute print) as well as a strip to hold LED light strip along the top rail. None of these parts are required but they make the machine easier to manage and keep clean from filament pieces. The lights are required for me since I keep this in a dark corner of the room.

Updated Summary: Awesome machine and no I don't work for and am not affiliated with TenLog but they treated me great with the damaged extruder and the machine works really well so doing this review for people that are interested in an IDEX machine - for the price you would be hard pressed to find a better IDEX machine.

October 6, 2022
Update Updated Summary: OK, I've been using this machine almost everyday since I bought it almost a year ago. Just a fantastic machine, not the fastest but it works great. I've been printing with combinations of various filaments without any issue - multiple colors of PLA, TPU, PetG and several others. I don't use ABS anymore but have been printing with ASA and if the model isn't too big the machine handles it fine. I've printed models that are at the limits of the machines capacity and it has delivered consistently. The mixing of colors in prints is really nice especially since I create various types of control boxes and panels that have words on them - I use black for the structure and (typically) gold for the wording and I am amazed every time. I have had a weird issue that happens maybe once a month where the right printhead gets confused at the beginning of the print and comes all the way over but then stops with a message saying "can't print model" (or something like that). When this happens I simply turn it off and back on and everything is fine, maybe a limit switch issue - not sure but it really hasn't been a problem - it never has happened during actual printing.

So, this is my last update. Great machine, great customer service - definitely recommend it to anyone wanting an inexpensive but high quality IDEX 3D printer. All modes work well, mirror and duplicate are fantastically convenient for many of the things I create. Good luck and enjoy!
top_rant.title: Great IDEX Printer

top_rant.text:
First off, this is an excellent printer. I go into my local 3d printing shop and see all of the $1000+ printers there and see their prints next to them and say mine's better than that, better than that one, better than this one, etc. I've been very pleased with my purchase, I have had it for about 3-4 weeks and have printed all kinds of things, large things and small, and print jobs that were over 40 hours. The print bed size is very generous and you can print multiple parts right up to the edges with ease. The printer is very sturdy and the build was fairly easy to do for anyone who can follow instructions, which are very detailed with a few exceptions. So here are my recommendations after having built and used the printer extensively.

Build:
1. The "T" nuts that slide into the aluminum rails (called 20x20 extruded aluminum rails) are designed to twist on an angle and lock into place. I tried at first sliding them down the rail and quickly found out that most don't fit and realized I was doing it wrong and that you press them in parallel to the rail's groove. You do have to put in the bolt and twist lightly until you know the nut has rotated as opposed to being pulled up by the bolt, in which case you will know because of how it feels when it tightens. It will be tight very quickly and have no give, as opposed to when the nut has rotated to an angle and is gripping correctly. There were times with certain locations that I had problems getting the nut to rotate, so I had to check down the rail's groove with a light to make sure the nut had twisted 20-45 degrees from parallel to the groove.
2. Pay attention to direction, I mounted the y-axis motor and bed opposite from where it was supposed to be. I ended up having to rotate the frame 180 degrees instead of flipping that and it was a pain. Easily avoidable.
3. Get a caliper or ruler with mm on it, you will absolutely need one. A tape measure will work but is a but more difficult to work with.
4. Where it asks you to make sure things are flush, make sure they are.
5. Use the provided cord ribbon to wrap the cables from the print head, you don't need zip ties or anything else and run the cable over the top of the frame or mount the lcd slightly away from the left lead screw bracket to provide a little gap for the cable to sit. The pictures of the printer here show it installed like that but the instructions don't call for it.
6. Auto Bed Leveling - This is a shortcoming in the documentation. Hictop does have a video on how to do leveling and there are some good videos on youtube that show you how to do it. Absolutely you will need to learn how to do this before you print. Basically you do an Auto Home procedure, see how far the print head is from the bed (or if it's on the bed), then you select Move Axis by 0.1mm increments on the Z-axis to lift the head up. You then unscrew the sensor's screws and pull it up on it's bracket until the red light is off, then push it down until the red just barely goes on. Then you tighten the screws back down, making the red light is still lit but that the sensor hasn't slipped down further. You want it to engage in the exact spot so that there is a gap enough for a sheet of paper to slide under the print nozzle. Oh, and yes, when you adjust on the z-axis (before moving the sensor), put it at a height to where a sheet of paper will just fit under with a tiny bit of resistance. After you have the z-sensor activating at the right height of a sheet of paper distance between the print head and the bed, disable the steppers (motors) and manually move the print head and bed to each of the corners and adjust the bed screws so that the print head is again at the same height, just high enough for a sheet of paper to slide under.
7. Power switch - There is no power switch on the machine. You can introduce one and print a cover for the switch and the power cable, OR do what I did and buy a small power strip and plug it into that, using the power strip's switch to turn it on and off.
8. X-axis rods - Use a level and manually adjust the hight of right or left x-axis bracket by twisting the lead screw so the top guide bar is level. If you don't, you will have to compensate on your bed, wondering why one side or corner requires you to raise the screw a LOT more than the others. I did this and it was because the x-axis rod wasn't level, the right bracket was essentially lower than the left one.
9. Y-Axis belt mount - This is a very important one! My belt mount came unscrewed and fell off twice when I was printing. They have you put two nuts so that the second one locks the first one down. I didn't have them tight enough, then I tried lock washers and it still came unscrewed. Solution - I printed a new belt mount from Thingiverse that was beefier and gave more room for the belts to wrap back around, then I used two nuts again with LOCTITE. Absolutely do get some Loctite to lock those nuts into place, the blue kind, which is removeable. Hasn't budged since.
10. The build is a bit time consuming because you have to assemble pretty much everything and the extruded aluminum with the t-nuts require a bit of meticulous focus. Probably took me 15+ hours, though I could definitely build one quicker now that I am familiar.

OTHER CONSIDERATIONS / Upgrades

1. While the print quality is (surprisingly?) good, I have an e3d Titan extruder and v6 hot end with micro-swiss nozzles ready to install to get even better prints. Requires printing a new bracket and firmware configuration changes.
2. Firmware - I am not sure which version of Marlin they are using, but it does have the automatic bed leveling installed and is actually pretty good. I am still going to update to the latest to see what changes I get but the one it comes with isn't bad at all. A few gripes with it though... it doesn't save your settings into the EEPROM after powering off. The knob isn't calibrated correctly for pulses per stop, so it clicks but doesn't always move down one in the menu. This can be fixed in firmware.
3. Software/Slicer - I have tried Cura, Repetier, and Simplify3D. While the first two are free and Cura is just fine, Simplify3D is amazing. Cura and Repetier are slicers. Repetier is also a print monitor, and like it, Simplify3D also does slicing and monitoring. S3D allows you to have per part print settings, so you can have two parts on the bed with different speeds, one with supports one without, different infills, etc.
4. Print Adhesion - The tape they give you is crap and is really difficult to get your prints off of. If you are going to use tape, get blue painters tape (3M is really good). The green frog tape didn't work very well I found and I haven't used Kapton. Having said that, what you really want to get is a PEI sheet. They sell a 12x12" sheet on Amazon for about $20, from a seller called Gizmodorks. This made all the difference in the world. I had constant problems with warping with ABS (considering there is no enclosure), especially on the corners. Once I put the PEI sheet on my bed, EVERYTHING sticks like none other, absolutely no warping at the bed with ABS, prints stick incredibly well, better than anything else I tried for adhesive/tape combos. This is IMO the only required upgrade that you need. PLA will print just fine without it, but ABS, PC-ABS, and others that require a high bed temp will cause issues with larger objects. BTW, ABS prints and looks beautiful, the walls are completely clean layers are barely detectable.
5. Settings/Printing Tips - I have been printing PLA at 200-210 with 60 degrees on the bed. If you are using blue painters tape, a brim is useful for adhesion. Strong hair spray on top of that will make it stick incredibly well. ABS I run at 230/110. PETG at 230/60. PC-ABS at 270/115 (you have to up the max temp in firmware or set the temp in the slicer, because 260 is normally the limit though it will easily go higher. 280 would probably be the max you would want to print at). 200 microns looks good enough for most things and is a great trade-off for quality/speed. I usually print at 60 or 80mm/s, with underspeeds of 60-80% for first/last layers and for outlines (settings in Simplify3D so it prints 60-80% of your set print speed, so for example, if set at 60 mm/s at 80% underspeed, your bottom layer will print at 48mm/s). If you are getting waviness in the outer walls, try lowering the accelleration and XYJerk setting in the LCD menu from 3000/20 to 1000/15. It will slow the print down a tiny bit but seemed to have a really good effect.
6. Marlin firmware - to upgrade the firmware you need to get the firmware from the manufacturer and save it. Write down all your settings from the LCD menus. Then update the configuration.h file in the new version of Marlin to match what is in the configuration.h in the current version. Hictop will email you the firmware if you ask. I emailed them when it was night time in China and got a response their next morning.

In conclusion, I have no problem in whole-heartedly recommending this printer. Features for the money is great! Feel free to follow up with questions and I can try to answer.
top_rant.title: GREAT BUY!  Well worth the $ and prints incredibly well!  TIPS/REVIEW

top_rant.title: My GREAT THANKS TO THIS PRODUCT IS BEYOND WORDS

top_rant.text:
First saw one of these when in a hotel. I was awkwardly dragging a bunch of luggage down to my car because I couldn't find a hotel luggage cart. When I do this I always have to make three trips. On the third trip and final trip, my wife and I not only have to drag more luggage down, but also mind our two year old at the same time. So, I see this guy with what I believe now to be a rocknroller multicart (I think now it was an R2) wheeling his luggage along with a big commercial speaker. It had those distinctive yellow wheels. I thought, man, this is cool. I had never seen one and thought man, I need something like that. I started looking at small luggage carts online and eventually came to the rocknroller. I didn't know they had so many. We go on many trips a year and lugging the luggage to and from the car for a whole family was a pain. It is awkward clumsy and always takes me about three trips. Not anymore. I take it everywhere now on our trips. Though small for what it is (I have two, the R6 and R10, but use the R10 mostly) usually my 4Runner is packed and I had no room for the cart in my SUV so I welded a hitch receiver to the cart and now it goes on the hitch which gives me more room in the cargo area of the SUV. Check out the pics at the end of the review.

FYI, the description on Amazon is wrong. They no longer have pneumatic wheels.

Pros:
1. Price. Good value for the price. There are similar ones out there, but they are a lot more expensive or the build quality is inferior from what I've read. There seem to be some knockoffs.
2. Compact. This is the best feature of these carts, the ability to collapse into something a lot smaller than their full size. I bought the R6 initially and it is pretty compact when folded, but when extended, it does have a decent amount of room (42 inches). I bought the R10 as well before the R6 came in thinking I wouldn't have enough room with the R6. I thought I'd return the R6 when the R10 came in but return shipping was more than half what I paid for it so I decided to keep it and now I have two of them! Costly mistake on my part. Turns out the R6 had more than enough space for what I needed. For something that folds down so small it hauls a lot of stuff. The R10 is even bigger. 52" vs 42" of the R6.

3. Ok build for the price. This is made in Malaysia, not Germany. So don't expect perfection and German made exactness, materials, and tolerances. If it was made in Germany, this cart would be a $500.00 cart. It is decently built. The biggest downfall of the construction/materials is the use of plastic bearings on the rear wheel and a non steel hub!? How can something be expected to carry 500lbs on a regular basis (that's what it is rated) on plastic bearings? I'm waiting for mine to fail so I can replace the rear wheels. I hear the front casters aren't much better. Haven't had any issues with those though. It does what I got it to do which is haul luggage to and from hotel room to car. Thankfully, the wheels and casters can be easily replaced with better quality ones if need be.

4. Seems pretty strong. It's hefty and seems solid. I don't know if the 500lb rating they attribute to this is true however. Seems unlikely to me but that's just an assumption based on my opinion of the construction. I haven't tested it to 500lbs and I'm unlikely to based on the way I use it. If you are going to be putting something heavy on it when fully extended, remember that the weak point is where the two metal tubes meet. I'd put a board on the cart to give it more rigidity at that crucial point. I don't know if these wheels and casters are rated for 500lbs either. I don't think with plastic bearings and plastic hubs, they could handle it. Again, conjecture on my part. It does exceed my needs of hauling luggage, cases of beer and water, etc when going on vacation. This is not a industrial/commercial heavy duty item. It does what it's supposed to well. It has saved me from going to the car 3x to unload it to bring stuff to the hotel room and vice versa. It's my own little luggage cart. People at the hotels seem a little jealous as they haul all their crap uncomfortably and awkwardly to and from their room like I used to do. It's not always easy to find a hotel luggage cart. They are always taken but I always have one.

5. Super easy to assemble. All you need is some pliers and an adjustable wrench. Takes less than 5 minutes literally. If you can't build this thing in less than 5 minutes turn in your man card. Some people have complained that the instructions aren't clear? I don't get it. They can't be clearer. Even without instructions, this is basic basic stuff. You can look at it and know how it goes together. You are only putting on the wheels. Nothing else. All you need is a basic pliers and an adjustable wrench. Even if you don't have an adjustable wrench you can do it with just pliers.

6. No need to buy the shelf and bottom shelf for this thing if you are slightly handy. Just build them yourself. For the top shelf I put in some 3 inch machine screws in the already drilled holes to act as shelf pegs. I choose to make a one size top shelf of 36x20 inches. I attached hooks at the 4 corners of the board that acts as shelf and they just hood onto the machine screws that are in the handle. . For the bottom, just drop in your own piece of cut board if you want. You can screw in some pieces of wood under the board to fit between the rails of the cart so it doesn't move around. The weight of your load will keep it down securely. I cut my bottom board also at 36x20 inches so it matches the top board. If the cart is extended to the total length of 52 inches, it's a bit short, but that's ok by me. The bottom board gives the cart some additional strength as well when fully extended. Remember the board has to fit in your car trunk/back area. That's why I didn't make a 52 inch shelf and bottom base.

7. Ok..I'm going to be ultra picky. This thing is a great concept. It could be really outstanding if 1) they used better thicker steel with thicker welds (the largest and most expensive models do have thicker steel). It would be great to have an aluminum version. ; 2) wheels with metal hubs, metal bearings, and better quality casters. 3) Make the side handles extendable/collapsible in the same way the cart collapses so you can adjust the height of the handle. After all this, yes it would be heavier, but that's what the public wants. Make this ultra heavy duty model in the small sizes and just paint them a different color. Have a heavy duty R2, R6, R8, etc. I'd pay the difference in price and most would too I think. The advantage that RR has over other carts is the collapsing capability, ability to extend, and relative compactness. What they are truly lacking to corner the collapsible Dollie market is superior build quality. People will pay more for quality. Make a better one..call it the RocknRoller Pro or something like that. Again, this is me being ultra critical. This is a very good product as is, for the price, and for what it is designed to do.

Cons:
1. Guess it could be made a little better but if it was, the R6 would not be $95.00 and the R10 would not be $155.00 shipped. The commercial grade carts used in the sound/music industries are like $500-1000.00 dollars. You can buy 3 of RocknRoller's top model for $1000.00. So not really a con. The best thing they could do to make this better is improve the hub and bearings from plastic to metal. Cmon guys....the public will pay the extra $. Is it because they make money selling replacement parts?

2. People complain that the cable used to release the handle is hard to use. This is simply not true. Some people have absolutely no mechanical skill at all. A child could use this thing.

Update: 8/16/17 After having my cart close to a year, it is still holding up well. I used it about 10 times in the year for trips and for moving heavy things in the yard. The only issue it is having is that the paint is chipping off and where it chips off it rusts pretty fast. I just use some touch up pain to cover those areas. Not for aesthetics, but to prevent further rusting. This is not that big of a deal unless the rust is affecting the welds. Tires and wheels have held up fine.

Update: 7/26/18 Had the R10 and the R6 for almost two years now. No problem with either. I used the R10 to move some pretty heavy metal cabinets around and it handled it fine. Also use it to carry cases of water from the car to the garage when I buy in bulk, use it for carrying gardening stuff, etc. I use these carts alot. Use them mostly for trips and hauling luggage from the car to our room. I don't like using the bellhops or valeting. I'm one of those guys. This is one of the best tools I've ever bought. Has made hauling tasks a lot easier. I hope the company reads these posts and makes the carts better. There's room for improvement by having making everything more heavy duty. People will pay for quality.

Update: June 21, 2019. Have had my two carts for 3 years. No problems with either at all. Find myself using the smaller one more often. We do a lot of car trips and hauling all our stuff from the car to the hotel room and back is a breeze. No more multiple trips or awkward and heavy carrying. The larger one has been useful for moving bigger stuff like furniture cabinets, TVs, big boxes, etc.

top_screaming.text: READ ALL OF THE REVIEWS. YOU ARE NOT BUYING A TOP SHELF PRODUCT. SO, IF YOU DO SMALL PROJECTS, IT IS SUPERB. I USE MINE FOR DIY E JUICE. THE MOST I RUN ON IT IS 180ML AS PG AND ESPECIALLY VG IS VERY THICK. FOR DIY E JUICE, I RECOMMEND THE SAME AS ANOTHER REVIEW, START WITH THE SPINNER (COMES WITH ONE) IN THE BEAKER. ADD 50ML JUICE AND TURN ON. SLOWLY ADD THE REST OF YOUR MIXTURE, AND IT WILL PULL AN 80ML DEEP VORTEX. START OUT BY CENTERING YOUR SPIN BAR, AND THEN SLIDE BEAKER AROUND TO FIND A GOOD DEEP VORTEX. THE SPINBAR THAT COMES WITH IT IS ONE INCH LONG. I USE A ONE AND ONE HALF INCH SPINBAR, SOLD ON AMAZON, AND I FIND IT WORKS BETTER IN THICKER SUBSTRATE. THE LARGER SPINBAR WILL CAUSE THE BEAKER TO HUM A BIT, BUT I PUT A TUPPERWARE BOWL ON THE BEAKER, AND A BARGLASS ON TOP OF THAT. THE HUM WILL CAUSE THE BEAKER TO MOVE AROUND, BUT NOT A LOT. I DO NOT THINK IT WOULD COME OFF THE INSTRUMENTS MAGNETIC FIELD, BUT, I COVER MY E JUICE EVERY TIME, DO NOT WANT TO BREATHE IN DUST. SOME REVIEWS SAID IT HUMS AFTER SHUT OFF. MINE DOES NOT. YOU HAVE TO TURN THE SPEED SWITCH COUNTER CLOCKWISE UNTIL IT CLICKS OFF. THE BOOK SAYS TO UNPLUG IT WHEN DONE FOR THE DAY, I DO, AND HAVE NO NOISE AT ALL. REMEMBER FOLKS, THIS IS NOT A HEAVY DUTY SPINNER. IF YOU NEED A HEAVY DUTY ONE, THEN FORK OUT THE BUCKS AND GET A COUPLE OF HUNDRED DOLLAR ONE ALSO ON AMAZON. SOME REVIEWS TALK ABOUT DURABILITY ISSUES, SPECIFICALLY SHORT LIFE SPAN. TOO MANY PEOPLE THROW STUFF OUT WHEN IT NO LONGER RUNS. GONE IS THE DAYS WE USED TO PUT IT ON THE BENCH, DIAGNOSE, AND FIX IT OURSELVES. IT IS SIMPLE INDUCTANCE. THAT IS, ELECTRICAL ENERGY STORED IN A MAGNETIC FIELD. IF YOU HAVE EVER TAKEN AN ELECTRONIC DEVICE APART AND SEEN HOLLOW RINGS WOUND WITH COPPER, THAT MY FRIEND IS AN INDUCTOR. YOU CAN READ ALL ABOUT IT ON A GOOGLE SEARCH. YOU CAN BUY TORROID CORES AND COPPER WIRE ON AMAZON. THINGS DO WEAR OUT. THINGS ARE EASY TO FIX. HECK, IT EVEN HAS A ONE YEAR WARRANTY. AN INDUCTOR IS MADE WITH A TORROID CORE, WRAPPED WITH A SPECIFIC GAUGE OF COPPER WIRE, EVEN COLORED COPPER WIRE. AN INDUCTOR GETS ITS STRENGTH BY THE NUMBER OF WINDS OF WIRE. WHEN MINE GOES DOWN, AND IT WILL, LOOK ON THE BRIGHT SIDE, YOU HAVE A CASE, A POWER SUPPLY, SWITCH, ETC. ANYTHING CAN BE FIXED. I AM SELF TAUGHT WITH USING THE INTERNET. SO, BOTTOM LINE, GREAT PRODUCT, GREAT PRICE, USE IT FOR WHAT IT CAN HANDLE. IF IT CANNOT HANDLE YOUR NEED, YOU NEED TO GET A MORE HEAVIER DUTY ONE. MAN, I FEEL GREAT. GOTTA GET THE WORD OUT ABOUT NOT JUST GIVING UP, NOT USING THE WARRANTY, AND NOT FIXING YOUR OWN STUFF. THE INTERNET HAS ANYTHING YOU NEED TO LEARN.
top_screaming.title: GREAT FOR DIY E JUICE

top_screaming.text: I BOUGHT THESE FOR REPAIRS TO THE DRIP SYSTEM IN MY YARD. THE MAIN LINE IS A 1/2" POLY PIPE WHICH FEEDS THE SMALLER 1/4" PIPES AT THE INDIVIDUAL LOCATIONS. I INTIALLY ORDERED THE #4 SIZE, WHICH WOULD GO AROUND THE PIPE, BUT WHEN A FITTING WAS INSERTED, THE CLAMPS WERE TOO SMALL. I THEN ORDERED THE #8 SIZE, BUT FOUND THEM TOO LARGE. I FINALLY GOT THE CORRECT SIZE IN THE #6 CLAMPS WHICH FIT LIKE THEY SHOULD.THERE SEEMS TO BE A VARIANCE IN WHAT A PARTICULAR # SIZE IS AS I HAVE BOUGHT OTHER BRAND CLAMPS IN THE #4 SIZE WHICH FIT CORRECTLY, BUT NOT THESE. ANYHOW, THE #6 CLAMP FROM THIS MANUFACTURER IS CORRECT FOR DRIP SYSYEMS. IT IS IMPORTANT TO REMEMBER THAT NOT ALL CLAMPS ARE CREATED EQUAL. MOST OF THE CLAMPS SOLD USE STAINLESS STEEL BAND,BUT A COMMON STEEL SCREW. THEY ARE USELESS IN A DRIP SYSTEM EXPOSED TO THE WEATHER AND UNDERGROUND USE. WITHIN A YEAR YOU WILL FIND THE SCREW HAS CORRODED MAKING THE CLAMP UNRELIABLE AND DIFFICULT IF NOT IMPOSSIBLE TO REMOVE. THESE ARE THE CLAMPS YOU WILL FIND PRICED AT 25-35 CENTS APIECE. THESE CLAMPS USE A STAINLESS STEEL SCREW AND BAND WHICH CAN TOLERATE THE WEATHER AND UNDERGROUND USE WITHOUT CORRODING. CLAMPS WITH STAINLESS STEEL SCREWS AS WELL AS BANDS ARE TWO TO THREE TIMES MORE EXPENSIVE THAN THOSE WITH STEEL SCREWS, BUT THESE WERE REASONABLY PRICED AND WELL WORTH PAYING SLIGHTLY MORE FOR THE PEACE OF MIND THAT THEY WILL STILL BE INTACT FIVE YEARS LATER IF YOU HAVE TO DIG THEM UP. A COMPARABLE CLAMP AT HOME DEPOT OR LOWES WILL RUN YOU ABOUT $1.00 APIECE, SO AT 44 CENTS EACH, THEY ARE A GOOD ALTERNATIVE.

I WAS REALLY LOOKING FORWARD TO GETTING AND USING THIS PASTURIZER AND I HATE TO GIVE BAD REVIEWS BUT I HAVE BEEN VERY DISAPPOINTED WITH THIS PRODUCT ESP. GIVEN THE HIGH PRICE THAT I PAID. THE INSTRUCTIONS APPEAR TO HAVE BEEN WRITTEN FIFTY YEARS AGO AND HAVE BEEN RE-PRINTED SO MANY TIMES THEY ARE ARE NOT VERY CLEAR.
THE PRODUCT DESIGN IS REALLY BELOW AVERAGE. THE HANDLE ON THE INTERIOR MILK CONTAINER DOES NOT EASILY FIT INTO THE OUTER CONTAINER ESP. WHEN THE INNER CONTAINER LID ATTACHMENT IS ENGAGED. THE ATTATCHMENT TO HOLD THE INNER LID DOWN IS VERY DIFFICULT TO ATTACH AND UNATTACH LEADING TO ALOT OF FRUSTRATION BEFORE PASTURIZING AND BURNT FINGERS AFTER THE HOT MILK IS PASTURIZED.
THE UNIT SEEMED TO HEAT TO THE CORRECT PASTURIZING TEMPATURE AND THEN SOUND THE ALARM THE FIRST TIME, BUT SINCE THEN IT HAS NOT AND TRYING TO RECALABRATE IT IS A NIGHTMARE. THE TINY HOLE IN
WHICH YOU ARE SUPPOSED IN INSERT A SCREW DRIVER TO CHANGE THE SETTING IS SO SMALL THAT YOU NEED A TINY SCREW DRIVER WHICH NOT EVERYONE MIGHT HAVE, LUCKLY I DID.
SINCE THE TEMPATURE SETTING IS RECESSED INTO THE UNIT THERE IS NO WAY OF KNOWING IF THE SCREWDRIVER HAS MADE CONTACT OR HOW MUCH THE SCREW HAS BEEN TURNED. SO RECALABRATING IS TOTALLY HIT AND MISS-MOSTLY MISS. THE HOSE THAT IS SUPPOSED TO FILL THE OUTER UNIT WITH HOT WATER DOES NOT FIT ON MY FAUCET CORRECTLY RESULTING IN WATER SPRAYING EVERYWHERE AND I HAVE YET TO FIGURE OUT WHAT THE LARGE BLACK HOSE IS FOR. I HAVE ABANDONED THE HOSES, THE LID ATTACHMENT AND AM RELYING ON ON MY THEROMETER AND NOT THE ALARM TO SIGNAL WHEN THE MILK IS HOT ENOUGH TO BE AT THE CORRECT TEMPATURE, AT LEAST UNTIL I CAN GET THE CALABRATION RIGHT. THIS PASTURIZER IS BASICALLY A GOOD IDEA AND BETTER THAN A DOUBLE BOILER TO PASTURIZE MILK BUT IT NEEDS TO BE TOTALLY RE-DESIGNED AND THE INSTRUCTIONS UP DATED AND RE WRITTEN.

top_exclaim.text: [[VIDEOID:5770035f9b47009ed5ec65e5fba96ea2]] I've been trying to remove latex paint from my kitchen cabinets for forever. Paint stripper takes forever, it's a mess to clean up and it's a pain to deal with the gooey paint afterwards. I've got an oscillating tool but the sandpaper packs up with the latex super fast so you're burning through those. I thought I would give these a try and holy crap I am in love. They do remove a decent amount of base material so you have to be careful but these things take the paint off super easy. In my video I'm just removing a little bit of paint with one of these abrasive wheels that I've been using for probably an hour or two. I probably won't need more than 10 of these to finish my entire kitchen, they last forever. I am beyond excited, I have been trying to figure out a way to do this that wouldn't take me 6 months to get everything removed. Granted, these are small so I'm not doing large surfaces but for the edges, rounded corners, and little areas, these things are a gift from God. Now that I know how well they work for what I'm using them for, I would have paid $20 for one wheel. I'm so freaking excited, now I have to go find some that are a lot bigger to do the main areas. IM SO EXCITED!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
top_exclaim.title: OMG THESE ARE THE ABSOLUTE BEST THING EVER!!!!!!!

top_exclaim.text:
This is an amazing product to use for whitening your teeth... I have been using Opalescence for sever years now, and will continue using this for the rest of my life!!!

I first started using this product when i got my braces off... (thanks mom & dad for getting me braces)
Once i started i just couldn't stop!!! Not only does this product make you have "MOVIE-STAR WHITENESS", but combine it with a perfectly straight smile, and everyone will thing that you have veneers..
And for me, I LOVE THAT kind of result/feeling!!! (((I cant tell you how many times i have people comment on my teeth!!! Maybe 30 ppl a week!!!!!!

***A few things people need to know about using 35%.... or any other percentages.... ***

Gum sensitivity - Yes this product will cause pain should you put it on your gums.... (HELLO?!?!?! This is made for your TEETH not your GUMS!!!)
If you have bleach on your gums it means that you put too much in your tray!!! So you should immediately use a tissue and wipe all excess bleach OFF!!!

Most people put a lot because they want to whiten the tartar stains around the gum line... You can achieve whitening that area by bleaching for 2-3 days, then go to your dentist and have them do a "Hygiene/cleaning" and then continue your bleaching process. This will help break up the tartar so the Hygienist will be able to get more of the tartar off. Thats my secret. ;o)

Now like me, i tend to use 1 syringe for both top and bottom trays. With a little left over for the next day application. So with an 8 pack, you should have about 10 Applications. You may have more depending how much you use.

Now, tooth sensitivity....
Yes, 35% will cause the most sensitivity..... Your better off with using the 20%!!! A good thing to remember is that you should brush your teeth with a tooth sensitive tooth paste... Before and after your bleach!!! Not a whitening one!!! Dont over do it!!!

I believe Colgate has a new tooth sensitive one out... I have not used this one but seems to be really good. (I use armor-hamor advanced whitening)

*** If you like my review, or have any questions please feel free to contact me***
Remember keep on Smiling people!!!

PS.
I strongly recommend having professional whitening trays made!!!
Also, at 35% this is made for heavily stained teeth, and people that DO NOT have a problem with sensitivity!!! I used this once..... And for me, i prefer the 20% ;o)

I dont smoke, drink coffee, or eat or drink highly acidic foods.
If you do, thats fine... Just know that once a month you may need to re-whiten your teeth. One syringe will bring you back to your desired results.

Also, PROTECT YOUR TOOTH ENAMEL!!!! This is what the bleach WHITENS!!! If you destroy your enamel on your teeth, you will NEVER get it back, and or that "MOVIE STAR WHITENESS" You will have more of an opaque white color, versus a solid one.
If you drink, a lot of wine, orange juice, soda, NEVER BRUSH AFTER HAVING ACIDIC food!!!!! Just rinse your mouth out with water and chew on Trident!!! Its ADA accepted, and it also helps keep your teeth white!!!

If anyone works for trident, could you please give me a lifetime supply..... ((wink, wink))

top_exclaim.text:
'A Good and Superb PLA!'

Frankly Id heard many fine things and some favorable comment on SUNLU Filaments!
So having bought a roll an done a project I can Honestly Report that most of the favorable things reported are in truth correct as it is a 'Very Fine' Plastic and SUNLU definitely has its act together!!!.
PROS:
Makes a Neat an Classy looking Part!
Easy to Run, Easy to Set Up!
Its the Real Deal You Get A Full Roll of !st Class Filament!
Almost the Only Silk PLA Plus+ on the Market!!!!!
A Real Bargain as the Price is Fantastic for what you get!.
Fast Amazon Shipping cannot be Beat!!!!!
CONS:
None actually cept If Id known this was a PLA+ Id have ordered it sooner!!!!!

In General:
Now In My Useage The SUNLU worked and printed quite well.
Setup was simple, I use Sticky Blue painters tape.
Set Nozzle at 259 and Bed 109 and let warmup for 10-15 mins.
I set 1st Layer at Nozzle 257 Bed 100, Speed at 35 or 40!
Then after 1st layer is down and hard I set the rest of the run at about say:
Nozzle 250- or 255 Bed 60 0r 75 and let Speed run at 45...
True "I Know" at those settings Ill get some stringing but 'I dont have any problems with layers not bonding and It don't take 'All Day' to get the part finished either.
Yes I know there are all kinds of 'Micro-settings, But I know that most machines have there own 'Pet Settings for wanting to run right.
Now most folks want to run 'Cold and Fast' But I tried that and got a LOT of junk parts!.
For me I found that "Slow but Hot" seems to get 'Better Parts'.....
Me I adjust my speed with a New Plastic until Stringing starts to appear then I back off fast!.
I also run my tests a 'Bit Differently' as many will run a ducky or a benchly and its a good way.
For Myself I have a small but 'difficult part', See it dont take much Plastic but if the plastic is good enough
to make a 'Difficult Part' then the easy stuff will run Home Free as the SUNLU did!!!!!!!!!!
Now One Major Complaint w Sunlu is it doesnt extrude well or the teeth dont grip the Plastic right!.
Yes at one time 'I Had' that issue but here is how I worked around it!.

A HOT TIP:
Now sooner or later when the teeth wear down you will have to replace that drive extruder gear!.
On most new machines its the round Brass One w the two small allen nuts sticking out the top side!
Most guys re install the gear wrong, I know I did??????
Yes I did as most of us will do what the YouTube guys did as they 'Set It' Plumb in the middle!.
Hey if you are satisfied and it works for you go for it!!!!!!!!!!
But there's a Better Way, In stead of in the Middle I just slid the Gear down a notch and let the plastic
run off the teeth on the Top Side of the Gear as it grips better and runs smoother too.
Oh yes it will surely wear like they all do in time, But if you did it right there is no great need to replace the gear not by any means sic!!!...
Just loosen the allens then "CAREFULLY" slide the gear back upwards until the Teeth on the bottom which are still good are NOW gripping the plastic as I think its pretty neat to reuse and get good service from the same part twice!!!!!.
TO: Close;
SUNLU Silk PLA is super good stuff that is easy to set up, It runs fine and makes a good part!.
And to Finish Its the only PLA+ that runs a silk fibre so it has Very Considerable strength and I definitely
Intend to try some Printer Gears with this and the smooth finish makes a classy looking part too!.
Kudos to the Seller!
Kudos to Amazon!
Nuff Said...
IndianaED
top_exclaim.title: REALLY GOOD PLA

top_five_star_obscene.text:
My brother builds 100 million dollar satellites and uses this exact tape on the satellites. He gave me a roll of it and I cherished it as something special. It is very strong, sticks better than any duct tape I have ever used and is very cool because it is used for space machines. I have tried to find good duct tape at hardware stores like Home Depot, Lowes and Ace but none of them carry duct tape that is worth one cent regardless of how super strength they claim it is or what ever high price they charge for it. It's all crap. This stuff however rocks. When the roll by brother gave me was used up I got online and found it available to everybody and at not NASA or Military prices but at normal reasonable prices. I'm so glad I was able to get more without asking my brother to grab some from the clean room where he builds satellites.

Here is an example of how excellent this tape is. I put up some plastic sheeting in a client's garage in order to isolate my work space from his convertible sports car. I used some of this tape to adhere one vertical stretch to a very roughly textured cement wall then stopped because I did not want to use it all. It's special as I mentioned. I ran to Ace and asked for their best duct tape and they directed me to a Scotch brand duct tape, also made by 3M that is marketed as "professional" "super strength" and is was their most expensive. I used this Scotch duct tape to finish putting up the plastic sheeting on additional cement walls as well as several stretches of unpainted wood. The new duct tape did not last the day before it failed and came down from all surfaces while the 8979 satellite grade duct tape is still holding strong to the rough cement a full month later. Amazing stuff.

Why don't brick and mortar stores sell this? Why do I have to find it online and have it delivered if I want some? Are not the local stores interested in selling good quality duct tape that actually works? Why is crap tape so heavily marketed and expensive and why does it suck? Amazon has a market for this stuff from me. I despise you marketing gurus with your sell more crap for more money more often motto that is ruining everything! I remember my dad's duct tape just worked without question. Now I'm all bummed out. I'm going to go play with my space duct tape until I feel better. Maybe I can find a marketer to gag and bind with it. That would cheer me up.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Used for building 100 million dollar satellites!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Thank God for this vacuum! I have been begging my husband for a new one for over a YEAR and I finally couldn't stand it anymore... one more game of "chase the cheerio" and I would have gone crazy. I looked up a bunch of reviews, but I was leaning towards the Shark from the beginning. I admit I was put off a bit by some of the negative reviews, but went out with the kids the other day to Bed Bath and Beyond and just bought it. Full price. It has been worth every penny. I put it together very easily and had a BLAST vacuuming the living room. The amount of gunk that came up was enough to make me sick as I had JUST vacuumed with the old one that morning after the kids spilled dry cereal all over. OMG. I can't believe we were living in that filth. I just KNEW the old one was a piece of crap. Well the past two days have been a vacuuming frenzy in my house. And I'm emptying canister after canister of gunk. I feel so relieved to finally have a clean(er) house.

Some of the things people have complained about I haven't really seen as a problem. The cord length is fine. I'm able to get into a few rooms before unplugging and bringing it closer. The canister size is fine. I actually prefer it to be as small as it is, it forces you to empty it after each use. And really, why wouldn't you? I emptied it after cleaning each room, but I imagine with routine use, I'll probably be able to vacuum the whole house and just empty once. And if I have to empty a few times... so what? That's just that much more crap out of my carpets.

The amount of suction is awesome. And it's not that heavy either. It's comparable to my old one, but actually has power behind it, it's so easy to push and it's awesome! The thing is practically self-propelled! I also love the compact head. I am able to get into tight spaces and under tables etc without having to move too many things (chairs etc). The hose attachment is a bit awkward, but I honestly have only used it only a few times- this thing is able to pick up gunk along the baseboards just by pushing the head right up against the wall. I love that. I just feel like I'm able to whip around the house with this thing and I actually enjoy vacuuming now!

I will update in a few months as others have done. But honestly, even if this thing craps out in a year, I still feel like it's worth the money. For the amount of crap I pulled out of my carpets, it's like having a super expensive Kirby at Target prices.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Gotta love something that sucks!!!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Used these to put wheels on our kitchen trash can. They roll easily over the vinyl flooring, and carpet. Nice compact size, but heavy duty enough for my purposes.

In case you are curious, I cut a piece of 3/4 inch plywood the same shape and size as the bottom of the trash can to give it some weight, something to screw the casters onto and to attach the trash to. Painted it black like the trash can and screwed the trash can down. Worked great. Now my trash can rolls instead of scraping along on the floor.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Good quality, nice small size, roll smooth

top_five_star_obscene.text: Finally! I have been searching for years to find a reliable garbage can. I consistently buy garbage cans that crack and break and I have to attach the ever-dreaded "GARBAGE" sign with Scotch tape to the thing, hoping the garbage men will see it and haul the piece of decrepit junk away, rather than continuing to throw it back into my yard. Not this time! This garbage can is the real deal (and, funny enough, after buying this one, what do I see at nearly every neighbor on my block's driveway the following garbage day? The exact same garbage can! I wonder if they too got fed up with the cans you find in the local department stores and bought this can from Amazon.) I was disappointed that it was only plastic, but it is incredibly durable plastic that holds up against all kinds of weather and no matter what weight you put into it. The one thing I can never figure out, though, is why they don't put little holes on the bottom to let the rain out. These cans are heavy enough as it is without the addition of rainwater, but I guess that would compromise their strength.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Finally - a good garbage can!

top_five_star_obscene.text: This product is the best trash can you can have. We had the bigger one but then installed new cabinets and had to purchase the 16 gallon. The 16 gallon still  fits a 30 gallon trash bag like the 23 gallon one and seriously fits more trash than any other can. Plus side it isn’t bulky but it fits more trash than the bulky trash cans, also pulling out a garbage bag that has been jammed with trash is usually a struggle not for these they slide right out.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Just buy it it’s worth it!!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
The cleaning staff in our small office replaces about 50 trash bags a day. Even if you just throw a single sticky note in the trash, they take your trash bag and give you a new one. What a waste! This is just the right size for a day's worth of rubbish in my cubicle. For any sticky or wet waste, I walk that to my co-worker's trash can in the next cubicle or the office kitchen. The cleaners just dump my trash into another bag and I am not wasting any more trash bags.

I have matching black mesh pen cup and monitor stands, so it looks like it is part of a set.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Perfect for a cubicle warrior

top_five_star_obscene.text: These trash cans are very solidly built and hold a lot (55 gallons). My city has large trash bins for regular trash and recycling, but no bin for yard trash. In Spring, Summer, and Fall I have lots of yard trash, and a hard receptacle is one way the county allows us to use for placing at the curb for pickup. These cans are perfect for that purpose. They would also work well for regular trash. Given the large size, they may look large in small kitchens. The lid may have to be ordered separately depending on how the product is listed. These sturdy cans should last a long time with normal usage. While they may be in the sun a lot if used for pickup from the street, they should hold up to the sun or elements well. I recommend these trash cans.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Solidly Built Large Trash Cans

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Looking to by parts to fix my original machine that up and quit rotating the brush and not sure if it was the motor or a switch problem but not finding the part that was only there 4 months earlier for 54 dollars. I ran across Amazon's refurbished/like new models. I wanted this NV350 version either 351 or 352. I put in my to buy page the 352 for 62 dollars but when moments later I went to finish the order it was gone telling me it was no longer available. While just before that it stated it was and was a pre-order as they were comming in August 26. SOOO I ordered a different model. As nice as it is it is much heavier and though it does a good job on the plush and one brand new shag rug I have that this NV350 series does not work on...I missed the light weight and my love for this one. So I kept monitoring it through August 26 to see if the 352 was offered again. NOPE ended up buying this for about 20 dollars more and that is fine. Parts alone would add up to that if I needed more than just the motor head anyway. I will keep my original now as parts. I was tempted to take a star off as this one runs much louder than the original. I put it through a lot of work. I was sad to see the hepa filter is no longer the charcoal sponge that came with my original and I will actually switch the foam one back into this new machine.
It arrived in a non-manufacturers box. THough it is much like new there was a scuff on the front of the roller head and that is fine. The other machine I bought had nothing we could identify as any possible defect cosmetic or otherwise. The scuff is not a problem very minor and all parts were clean and prestine no sign of ever having been tried. A good deal at approx half price. I was tempted to buy two as our house is 3 levels not counting basement or attic. The orignal still sucks fine just no rotation. Which could be just the switch. My husband thought he'd look at it this winter to see if he can figure out if it is the switch or the roller motor. He's good at those things when he has the time and inclination both. Meantime this is my favorate vacuum almost of all time. (Years ago I saved a canister vacuum from the trash a neighbor threw out. It was a Kenmore and it had metal tool heads etc. I cleaned it inside and out. Replaced a few things and though bags were no longer available for it the long bags for uprights fit it just right so for over 30 years I used it and was amazed at it's power and how it cleaned. The fabric like hose frayed to beyond fixing so had to find a new one. A dyson the original style was CRAP very expemsive CRAP. The two others one very classic style to the original upright hover but Eureka went thorugh belts constantly. UGH. But it cleaned well. Then I bought a very expenisve over 300 dollar LG that had out of this world reviews. My only mistake was not packing it up instantly to return without trying. It was terrible. And it still remains re=packed into it's original box and maybe it'll stay with this house when and if we ever decide to move.......about here I took a chance on this little gem bought local on sale from the then Pamida store. OH MY GOSH this is my machine. Light enough to hold up on the stairs as I go up them. The removable basket that I can tote up the ladder to vacuum walls and dropped open beams from the ceiling is an added luxury. I do not know why it is louder and kind of squeals compared to my original but it appears to pick up fine and handle the same identical way. I had to replace the tiny little hose on the front right of the rotator head twice. The original did not want to stay on either but my husband fixed that. The next two replacements never gave me a problem with staying on. My only negatives are the head can catch under a few of our counters on heavy roll around kitchen cabinets that snag it and makes it difficult to get free. And so have to remember to handle it to avoid and now of course the higher pitched squeal. I remember years ago my mother in law looking for a light weight vacuum as she aged and this would have been the one for her too no doubt in my mind. We had the fancy kirbies in the day too. Not sure what happened to ours. I saved all their tools and often use them with this and a newer canister that's just ok as nothing to date compares with that trash saved one...which by the way had a super long flexible cord that automatically re-wound itself with a touch of your foot. Yes it's nice to have no bags to deal with but that vacuum even with the bags was superb in every way. Ok enough...I think there must be a cult following of this model line among all of Sharks machines. (PS I've looked into the ones that say dual action hard and rug flooring and no fly out the back of the vacuum head....and the one that is suppose to not collect hair and thread on the roller too. Reviews with photos clearly suggest these are gimmicks that to date do not work or function. I have long hair and also sew and I would love a machine that did not snag my hair winding and binding up the rollers and wheels. Not realistic to date.) I've been married 47 years and prior to that been doing all the house work in my family since age 12 so well over 57 years with the virtues and miseries both of vacuum cleaners.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Had to replace my original now 12 plus years old.

top_five_star_obscene.text: I love this trash can! It was a compromise with me and my husband (he didn’t want a standard small trash-we have too much trash) and I didn’t want the round 30gal trash can taking up so much space without a lid!! I did buy the lid that goes to this, I think it’s so stupid to buy separately and it’s basically the cost of the damn trash can itself! Insane!!!! But it works great (kid sometimes gets stuck) but it keeps the smell away (had crabs in there for 2 days and couldn’t smell!!) I more importantly love how much of a space savor it is in the house while still having a large trash can for a family of 8!!
top_five_star_obscene.title: So far it’s been great!!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
This little waterproof trash can is made of a smooth, easy-to-clean material so it can be wiped down quickly to stay attractive and modern-looking.

The infrared sensors open and close the little trash can without our having to touch anything, which is nice, particularly when hands are full of whatever we're about to throw out. While it is an attractive can, it is easy to hide and easy to store because it is small and fits into most of my lower cabinet spaces.

The trash can is also airtight so it holds in smells from food or other odorous trash. The garbage bags fit easily into the can and the top of the can is removable for easy emptying and cleaning.

What I like most about this little touchless trash can is that it opens and closes without a sound, and the detachable bucket ring holds the trash bag nicely in place so there is no slippage. Overall, we are quite pleased!

Also worth mentioning that it is rechargeable with the provided USB cable. No batteries to change. Yes!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Touchless trashcan works like a charm!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
this trash can is great. the lid part of the top I can leave open. it will lock when closed. and then the hinge part stays locked until I open it to remove the trash. I wanted a smaller trash can near our patio table to put the cans & small things in.
we throw the plates, etc. in the other trash can.
it doesn't get emptied every week like the larger trash can I have. I do put clear 55 gallon trash can liners in it with the flaps. I slide the back flap under the hinge and they stay perfectly. do not overload it or the trash can bag will tear upon removal.
it is a heavy durable outdoor plastic.
it doesn't turn moldy and green like most outdoor plastic stuff. itz very easy to clean !! I've had mine for nearly 5 years and it still looks brand new. best $40 I ever spent !!
very pleased. highly recommend.
top_five_star_obscene.title: 5 years & going strong !!
